file.content:
### `decree lint`

Validate all documents. Per-file checks: frontmatter validity, required sections. Cross-file checks: supersede symmetry, referenced documents exist. Exit 1 on any error.

Output format: `{filepath}: {message}` — one line per error, machine-parseable.

### `decree sprint`

Enable and manage optional sprint-scoped execution tracking. Sprint mode is off
until the repository has `decree/sprints/ledger.yaml`, created explicitly by:

```bash
decree sprint init "Sprint 1"
```

Once enabled, exactly one active sprint exists unless sprint mode is paused.
Use backlog or draft pool for work that should not enter the current sprint:

```bash
decree sprint status
decree sprint add SPEC-01KT22NMS0D19VMD8VPK4D2MNX
decree sprint add PRD-01KT22NMRTFTWFFARAN0PVEETA --kind planning
decree sprint backlog SPEC-01KT22NMS0D19VMD8VPK4D2MNX --reason "not ready for this sprint"
decree sprint draft-pool SPEC-01KT22NMS0D19VMD8VPK4D2MNX --reason "exploratory"
decree sprint pause --reason "summer freeze"
decree sprint resume "Sprint 2"
```

Close a sprint by providing an outcomes YAML file for every open active item:

```yaml
outcomes:
  SPEC-01KT22NMS0D19VMD8VPK4D2MNX:
    kind: carried_over
    reason: implementation started late
```

```bash
decree sprint rollover "Sprint 2" --outcomes sprint-outcomes.yaml
```

Completed outcomes are accepted only when the close-time acceptance-criteria
snapshot is 100% for primary criteria. Carryover is linear to the immediate
successor sprint and requires a reason.

### `decree index regenerate`

Regenerate per-type `index.md` markdown tables from frontmatter. Grouped by status.

### `decree index rebuild`

Rebuild `.decree/index.sqlite`, the derived query cache used by `decree why`,
`decree refs`, MCP tools, intent-review/check, health, and retrieval eval.
Git trailers with non-canonical IDs are reported as warnings and ignored; they
are not converted implicitly.

### `decree index status`

Show schema version, rebuild timestamp, and row counts for `.decree/index.sqlite`.

### `decree index verify`

Compare on-disk frontmatter with `.decree/index.sqlite`. Exit 1 when the index
is missing, stale, or missing a document. Run `decree index rebuild` to refresh.

### `decree progress`

Show progress summary across all document types. When sprint mode is enabled
and active, the default scope is the active sprint's execution/planning items.
Use `--corpus` to keep the old whole-corpus view. The output prints its scope.
For parallel work, prefer explicit scope flags:

```bash
decree progress --doc SPEC-01KT22NMS0D19VMD8VPK4D2MNX
decree progress --chain PRD-01KT22NMRTFTWFFARAN0PVEETA
decree progress --changed --base origin/main
decree progress --governs src/decree/parser.py
decree progress --sprint SPRINT-01KT22NMS0D19VMD8VPK4D2MNX
decree progress --backlog
decree progress --draft-pool
decree progress --all-sprints
decree progress --include-context
decree progress --corpus
```

Progress counts only primary checkbox sections. Deferred/out-of-scope sections
are reported separately so parallel work does not look blocked by explicit
future scope.

### `decree ddd`

Assess the current Decree Driven Development phase and print the next action.
The assessment includes a governance-drift hint — dead and suggested governance
counts (run `decree health` for detail). Scope it the same way as progress when
an agent or worktree owns one slice. With active sprint mode, the default scope
is the active sprint; use `--corpus` to assess every document.

```bash
decree ddd --doc SPEC-01KT22NMS0D19VMD8VPK4D2MNX
decree ddd --chain PRD-01KT22NMRTFTWFFARAN0PVEETA
decree ddd --changed --base origin/main
decree ddd --governs src/decree/parser.py
decree ddd --sprint SPRINT-01KT22NMS0D19VMD8VPK4D2MNX
decree ddd --backlog
decree ddd --corpus
```

### `decree report regenerate SPEC-01KT22NMS0KTWGNKB36RR7K0JR`

Regenerate completion report snapshots from current frontmatter and checkbox

content.text:
Recon tamam. Önce teşhis, sonra öneri — özet: **sorun kilitlenme değil, ledger'ın tek dosyalık global mutable aggregate olması.** Çözüm olarak ledger'ı dizine ayrıştırmayı (state + canlı üyelik başına bir dosya + kapanan sprint başına bir arşiv dosyası) öneriyorum.

## Teşhis: Ledger paralel çalışmayı tam olarak nerede engelliyor

`decree/sprints/ledger.yaml` sistemdeki tek **elle yönetilen, global, monolitik mutable state**. Decree'nin geri kalanı tam tersi doktrinle kurulmuş: karar başına bir dosya, türetilmiş indexler (`index.md`, `.decree/index.sqlite`) explicit regenerate ile. Ledger bu doktrinin istisnası ve dört ayrı mekanizmayla paralelliği serileştiriyor:

1. **Yazma hunisi:** Sprint modu aktifken `decree new spec` otomatik olarak aktif sprint'e kaydediyor (`commands/new.py:145`). Her worktree'de yaratılan her SPEC, aynı YAML listesine (`sprints[active].items`) append ediyor → merge'de aynı noktada garanti çakışma.

2. **Lint zorunlu kayıt invariantı:** `_missing_post_init_specs` (`sprints.py:888`) sprint-init sonrası yaratılmış terminal olmayan her SPEC'in aktif sprint / backlog / draft_pool'dan birinde olmasını *hata* olarak zorluyor. Pre-commit lint çalıştığı için ledger'a dokunmayı erteleyip merge'e bırakma seçeneğin yok — SPEC dosyası ile ledger girdisi aynı commit'te olmak zorunda. Yani her planlama branch'i global dosyaya bağlanıyor.

3. **"Tam olarak bir aktif sprint" invariantı** (`sprints.py:610`): paralel iş akışları kendi sprint'ini açamıyor; ve `rollover_ledger` (`sprints.py:462`) *bütün* açık item'lara outcome istiyor — bir akış, diğerinin item'ları uçuştayken sprint kapatamıyor.

4. **Aynı worktree'de yarış:** `load_ledger → mutate → save_ledger` deseni kilitsiz. `save_ledger` (`sprints.py:292`) tempfile + atomic replace ile *bozuk dosyayı* engelliyor ama *kayıp güncellemeyi* engellemiyor: aynı worktree'de iki eşzamanlı planlama session'ı yazarsa son yazan kazanır, öbürünün item'ı sessizce kaybolur.

Not: sprint ID'leri ULID (`generate_sprint_id`), yani ID üretiminde contention yok — sorun tamamen tek dosyada.

Bugünkü haliyle temiz bir workaround da yok: `--backlog`/`--draft-pool` da aynı dosyayı yazıyor, pause modu da `new spec`'i ledger yazmaya zorluyor. Yani "doğru kullanım" davranış değişikliğiyle çözülmüyor; veri yerleşimi değişmeli.

## Öneri: Ledger'ı dizine ayrıştır (tek dosya → single-writer dosyalar + türetilmiş görünüm)

Anahtar ilke: **invariantları gevşetme, çakışma yüzeyini küçült.** Lint'in katılığı değerli — pahalı olan, o katılığın tek paylaşılan dosya üzerinden işlemesi. Önerim (`decree.sprints.v2`):

```
decree/sprints/
  state.yaml                 # schema, mode, state, active, paused — sadece init/pause/resume/rollover'da değişir
  live/SPEC-<ULID>.yaml      # canlı üyelik başına BİR dosya: {scope: active|backlog|draft_pool, kind, source, added, reason, review_after}
  closed/SPRINT-<ULID>.yaml  # kapanan sprint başına BİR arşiv: items + outcomes + snapshots + carryover linkleri
```

Bunun paralellik özellikleri:

- **Worktree'ler arası:** `decree new spec` artık iki *yeni* dosya yaratıyor (doküman + `live/` item dosyası), paylaşılan hiçbir dosyaya dokunmuyor. Merge = dosyaların birleşimi, textual conflict imkânsız. İki worktree aynı dokümanı farklı scope'lara koyarsa aynı `live/SPEC-x.yaml` üzerinde çakışır — bu da zaten yüzeye çıkması *gereken* gerçek semantik çakışma. Duplicate-membership, tek-aktif-sprint gibi invariantlar merge sonrası lint'te aynen kalıyor.
- **Aynı worktree'de çoklu session:** farklı dokümanlar → farklı dosyalar → yarış yok. `state.yaml` geçişleri (pause/resume/rollover) nadir; onlara ucuz bir advisory lock (`.state.lock`, fcntl) eklemek yeterli.
- **Rollover bilinçli olarak single-writer kalıyor:** integration branch'te yapılan bir seremoni — `live/` dosyalarını tüketir, `closed/SPRINT-x.yaml` arşivini yazar, carryover'lar için yeni `live/` dosyaları açar. Sprint kapatmanın serileşmesi doğru; paralel olması gereken günlük add/create yoluydu.
- **İnce ama önemli bir tasarım seçimi:** `live/` item dosyası hangi sprint'e ait olduğunu *yazmasın*; "canlı item, merge anında aktif olan sprint'e aittir" semantiği kullanılsın. Böylece branch açıkken main'de rollover olursa, merge edilen item eski (kapanmış) sprint ID'sini işaret edip lint'i kırmaz — yeni aktif sprint'e düşer. Sprint aidiyeti tarihe ancak kapanışta (arşiv dosyasında) yazılır; carryover doğrusallığı da zaten sadece kapanış tarihçesinde anlamlı.

Uygulama tarafı hafif kalır çünkü domain modeli zaten temiz: `SprintLedger.from_raw`/`to_dict` ve tüm geçiş fonksiyonları immutable dataclass'lar üzerinde saf; değişen esas şey `load_ledger`/`save_ledger`'ın tek dosya yerine dizini okuyup yazması. `validate_ledger`, `select_sprint_scope`, `progress/ddd` scope'ları API seviyesinde aynı kalır. Geçiş için tek seferlik `decree migrate sprint-ledger` (v1 dosyasını dizine böler; `migrate ids`/`migrate governs` emsalleri zaten var).

## Değerlendirip önermediklerim

- **Üyeliği doküman frontmatter'ına taşımak** (`sprint:`/`bucket:` alanı, ledger tamamen türetilmiş görünüm olur): çakışmayı aynı derecede çözerdi ama mevcut ADR'nin "sprint kaydı execution gerçeğidir, governance/doküman gerçeği değildir" ayrımını deler — execution state'i karar dokümanının içine sızdırır. Dizin yaklaşımı aynı faydayı ADR'nin duruşunu bozmadan veriyor.
- **Çoklu aktif sprint / lane'ler** (worktree başına sprint): asıl ağrın contention, çoklu sprint istemek değil. Lane'ler rollover ve carryover invariantlarını çoğaltır, "sprint = mevcut taahhüt" anlamını sulandırır. Dizin ayrıştırması sorunu lane'siz çözüyor.
- **`.gitattributes merge=union`** ledger.yaml için: YAML yapısına kör bir yara bandı; iç içe listelerde sessizce bozuk dosya üretebilir. Reddettim.

Onaylarsan bunu decree'nin kendi akışıyla formalize edebilirim — ADR ("sprint ledger'ın dizine ayrıştırılması", mevcut `ADR-01KW1QGHBT...`'yi supersede ederek) + SPEC (v2 şeması, migrate komutu, lock). Nasıl ilerlemek istersin?

lastPrompt: su anda paralel gelistirme yapamiyorum cunku ledger engel oluyor. bunu nasil cozerdin?  architectural olarak dusunur musun? git worktreelerle calisabiliyoruz, ayni worktreede birden fazla planning yap…

content.text:
İki doğrulama da temiz çıktı: `get_project_root` `.git`'e değil `decree.toml`'a bakarak yürüyor (`config.py:25`) — yani her worktree kendi kökünü doğru çözer; `.decree/` (SQLite cache) gitignore'da — worktree başına ayrı cache, git'e hiç girmiyor. Şimdi simülasyon.

## Kısa cevap

- **3 worker worktree, kendi decree'lerini yazıp işaretleyebilir mi?** Önerdiğim v2 (dizin-ledger) ile **evet, çakışmasız** — bir eksikle: worker işi bitirdiğinde bugünkü semantikte "done" diyemiyor (aşağıda). Bugünkü v1 ile **hayır** — üçü de `ledger.yaml`'ın aynı listesine append eder, entegrasyonda 3 yönlü conflict garantidir.
- **Ayrı worktree'den orchestrator denetleyebilir mi?** **Evet, iki modda** — ve biri bugün bile çalışıyor.

## Simülasyon: 3 worker + 1 orchestrator (v2 üzerinde)

**T0 —** `main`'de sprint aktif. `state.yaml` + `live/` mevcut. A, B, C worktree'leri branch'lenir; orchestrator O ayrı worktree'de.

**T1 — her worker `decree new spec "..."`.** Her biri iki *yeni* dosya yaratır: kendi spec dokümanı + kendi `live/SPEC-<ULID>.yaml` üyelik dosyası. ID'ler ULID olduğu için dosya adları çakışamaz; ortak hiçbir dosyaya dokunulmaz. Lint'in zorunlu kayıt invariantı (`sprints.py:888`) her worktree'de lokal olarak sağlanır — worker'lar pre-commit lint'i geçer. (v1'de de lokal lint geçer; v1'in kırılma anı hep entegrasyondur.)

**T2 — çalışma ve işaretleme.** Checkbox'lar spec'in kendi dosyasında; `decree status ... approve` da sadece doc dosyasına yazar. Doc-ownership varsayımıyla (her worker kendi decree'si) çakışma sıfır. Worker kendi dilimini `decree progress --changed --base origin/main` ya da `--doc` ile görür. Aynı worktree'de iki planlama session'ı bile güvenli: farklı spec'ler = farklı dosyalar; ilk mesajdaki last-writer-wins yarışı tek dosya kalktığı için kayboluyor.

**T3 — worker A bitirdi.** Burada simülasyon bir boşluk yakalıyor: A primary AC'leri %100'e çeker ama **"tamamlandı"yı kayda geçiremez**. İki kural engelliyor: aktif sprint item'ı outcome taşıyamaz (`sprints.py:760`) ve terminal statüdeki (implemented) SPEC canlı item olamaz (`sprints.py:776`). Yani outcome yazmak da `status implement` çekmek de lint'i kırar; tamamlama bugün yalnızca rollover'da — yani single-writer anında — ifade edilebiliyor. Üç worker'ın ortasında biri bitirip diğerleri devam edecekse bu yetmez.

**T4 — orchestrator denetimi.** İki mod:

1. **Yerinde denetim (bugün bile çalışır):** worktree'ler sonuçta diskteki dizinler; O her worktree'nin dizininde `decree progress` / `lint` / `ddd` çalıştırır — `get_project_root` cwd'den yukarı yürüdüğü için her worktree'yi kendi projesi olarak görür. Bu üç komut SQLite cache istemez, markdown'ı doğrudan parse eder. Sınırı: her worktree'yi *izole* görür; worker'lar arası invariantları göremez.
2. **Entegrasyon görünümü (v2'nin açtığı kapı):** O, worker branch'lerini bir `integration` branch'ine merge eder. v2'de bu merge yapısal olarak conflict-free (dosya birleşimi); tek `decree lint` cross-worker invariantları denetler — iki worker aynı dokümanı sahiplenmişse aynı `live/` dosyasında gerçek bir çakışma olarak yüzeye çıkar, stale referans ve tek-aktif-sprint kontrolleri burada koşar. v1'de bu döngü her turda ledger conflict'i çözmek demekti; asıl şikâyetin buydu.

**T5 — kapanış.** Merge tamamlanınca O rollover'ı yapar: outcome'lar + `closed/SPRINT-x.yaml` arşivi + carryover'lar. Tek yazar, `state.yaml`'a yalnız O dokunur — sorunsuz. Uçuş sırasında O rollover yapmışsa bile "canlı item, merge anında aktif olan sprint'e aittir" kuralı worker branch'lerini kırmaz.

## Simülasyonun önerime eklettiği parça

**Item-seviyesi tamamlama: `decree sprint complete SPEC-X [--evidence ...]`.** Worker'ın kendi `live/SPEC-X.yaml` dosyasına snapshot'lı bir `completed` outcome yazar (ya da dosyayı `done/`'a taşır) — yine sadece kendi dosyası, yine çakışmasız. Karşılığında iki invariant güncellenir: "aktif item outcome taşıyamaz" → "outcome'lu item artık canlı sayılmaz" olur ve %100 primary AC kanıtı item kapanışında aranır; rollover da yalnızca hâlâ açık item'lara outcome ister. Terminal status geçişini (implemented) ister worker complete ile birlikte yapar, ister orchestrator entegrasyonda — ikisi de lint-tutarlı olur.

Bu ekle birlikte senaryonun tamamı — 3 worker'ın yazması, işaretlemesi, *bitirmesi* ve O'nun hem yerinde hem birleşik denetimi — uçtan uca tutarlı çalışıyor. İstersen ADR + SPEC'i bu haliyle (dizin-ledger + `sprint complete`) formalize edeyim.

content.text:
Tamam. Plan: **Faz 0 recon (paralel envanter) → tasarım dokümanı → decree dogfood (PRD/ADR/SPEC, eski ADR supersede) → çekirdek implementasyon → testler → dokümantasyon + help + changelog → adversarial review → uçtan uca doğrulama** (3-worktree senaryosu dahil). input.content:
# Sprint Ledger v2 — Design Document (single source of truth)

Status: DRAFT — finalized after recon workflow results land.
Repo: /Users/doruk/Desktop/SIDE_HUSTLE/decree

## 1. Problem

`decree/sprints/ledger.yaml` is the only hand-maintained, global, monolithic
mutable file in a system otherwise built on one-file-per-decision documents and
explicitly-regenerated derived indexes. It serializes parallel work through four
mechanisms:

1. `decree new spec` auto-enrolls into the active sprint → every worktree
   appends to the same YAML list → guaranteed merge conflicts.
2. Lint's enrollment invariant (`_missing_post_init_specs`) forces the SPEC file
   and its ledger entry into the same commit → no way to defer ledger writes.
3. Exactly-one-active-sprint + rollover-requires-all-outcomes → one stream
   cannot close a sprint while another stream's items are in flight.
4. `load_ledger → mutate → save_ledger` has no lock → same-worktree concurrent
   sessions lose writes (last-writer-wins; atomic replace prevents torn files,
   not lost updates).

Additionally, item-level completion is inexpressible mid-sprint: an active item
must not carry an outcome, and a terminal (implemented) SPEC cannot be a live
item. Workers can only max out checkboxes; "done" exists only at rollover.

## 2. Design principle

Do not relax invariants; shrink the contention surface. One file per unit of
change; single-writer files; semantic conflicts surface in post-merge lint,
which is where an orchestrator adjudicates.

## 3. v2 storage layout (schema `decree.sprints.v2`)

```
decree/sprints/
  state.yaml                # sprint-mode state; changes ONLY at init/pause/resume/rollover
  live/<DOC-ID>.yaml        # one file per live membership (uppercase canonical doc id + .yaml)
  closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml   # one append-only archive per closed sprint, written at rollover/pause
```

### state.yaml

```yaml
schema: decree.sprints.v2
mode: enabled
state: active            # or: paused
active:                  # present iff state == active
  id: SPRINT-<ULID>
  name: Sprint 2 - ...
  started: '2026-06-26'
paused:                  # present iff state == paused
  since: '2026-07-03'
  reason: summer freeze
```

### live/<DOC-ID>.yaml (unified membership record)

```yaml
document: SPEC-<ULID>     # must equal filename stem
scope: active             # active | backlog | draft_pool
kind: execution           # execution | planning
source: new               # manual | new | carryover | deferred
added: '2026-07-03'
since: '2026-07-03'       # backlog only (age tracking)
reason: ...               # required for backlog and draft_pool
review_after: '2026-08-01'  # optional, backlog only
carryover_from: SPRINT-<ULID>  # optional; scope=active carryover items only
outcome:                  # optional; PRESENT ⇒ item is no longer "live membership"
  kind: completed         # mid-sprint allowed: completed | dropped (carried_over/deferred/superseded remain rollover outcomes — confirm against recon of existing sprint subcommands)
  at: '2026-07-03'
  reason: ...             # required for all kinds except completed
  to_document: DOC-ID     # superseded only
  evidence: {commits: []}
  snapshot: {status, primary_done, primary_total, deferred_done, deferred_total}
```

Key semantic: **live scope=active items do NOT record a sprint id.** A live
item belongs to whichever sprint is active at integration time. Sprint
attribution is written to history only when a sprint closes (fold into
`closed/`). This makes worker branches robust to a rollover happening on the
integration branch mid-flight.

### closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml

Same shape as today's closed SprintRecord: id, name, status: closed, started,
closed, items (each with mandatory outcome + snapshot). Chronological order =
ULID lexicographic order of filenames.

## 4. Concurrency properties (target)

- Cross-worktree: `decree new spec` creates two NEW files (doc + live file);
  no shared file touched; merges are unions. Two branches claiming the same doc
  → same live path with different content → a real, visible git conflict on
  exactly one small file.
- Same-worktree concurrent sessions: distinct docs → distinct files. Live-file
  creation uses O_CREAT|O_EXCL (atomic exists-check). Item rewrites (complete)
  use tempfile + atomic rename.
- State transitions (init/pause/resume/rollover) take an advisory flock on
  `decree/sprints/.lock` (fcntl; graceful no-op fallback where unavailable,
  documented). They are rare orchestrator-side operations.
- Rollover stays deliberately single-writer (integration-branch ceremony).

## 5. In-memory model (sprints.py rewrite)

Replace the monolithic SprintLedger aggregate + whole-file save with:

- `SprintState` frozen dataclass ← state.yaml
- `LiveItem` frozen dataclass ← live/*.yaml (unifies v1 SprintItem-in-active,
  BacklogItem, DraftPoolItem)
- `SprintRecord`/`SprintItem` kept for closed archives
- `LedgerView` read aggregate: `{state, live: dict[doc_id, LiveItem], closed: tuple[SprintRecord,...]}`
  with helpers: `active_open_items`, `active_outcomed_items`, `backlog_items`,
  `draft_pool_items`, `live_membership()` (only outcome-less files count).

Reads (`load_view`) assemble the directory into a LedgerView. Mutations write
ONLY the affected file:

- `write_state(state)` — atomic replace under lock
- `create_live_item(item)` — O_EXCL; error if the doc already has any live file
  (including an outcome'd one: "record folds at next rollover")
- `rewrite_live_item(item)` — atomic replace (e.g. writing an outcome)
- `remove_live_item(doc_id)` — used only by fold (rollover/pause)
- `write_closed_sprint(record)` — new archive file

v1 reader survives ONLY inside the migrate path (`_load_v1_ledger`), plus a
detection guard: if `decree/sprints/ledger.yaml` exists, every sprint API call
raises "sprint ledger v1 detected; run `decree migrate sprint-ledger`".
`sprint_mode_enabled()` returns True if state.yaml OR legacy ledger.yaml exists
so lint surfaces the migration error instead of silently skipping sprint checks.

## 6. Operations

- `init` — create dir + state.yaml (error if state.yaml or v1 ledger exists).
- `add / backlog / draft-pool` — create one live file. Duplicate-membership is
  structural (one file per doc). Existing v1 move/remove subcommands (recon)
  are ported as scope rewrites / file removals.
- `complete DOC [--commit SHA ...]` (NEW) — verify snapshot primary ACs are
  100%, write outcome {kind: completed, at, evidence, snapshot} into the item's
  own live file. Doc status may go terminal before or after; lint stays green
  either way.
- `drop DOC --reason` (NEW, if not already present in v1) — same mechanics,
  outcome kind=dropped.
- `pause --reason` — all scope=active items must carry outcomes; fold them into
  a closed archive, remove their live files, state → paused.
- `resume "Name"` — state → active with fresh sprint id.
- `rollover "Name" --outcomes f.yaml` — outcomes file must cover exactly the
  OPEN active items (outcome'd ones are already resolved); fold all active
  items into `closed/<old>.yaml`; delete their live files; create carryover
  live files (source=carryover, carryover_from=old); state.active → new sprint.
- `status / list` — derived views over state + live + closed.

## 7. Validation changes (validate_ledger v2)

Kept (rebased onto the new layout):
- schema/mode checks; active⇒active block with id/name/started; paused⇒since+reason
- paused ⇒ no scope=active live files
- filename stem == document field; canonical doc id; document exists in corpus
- kind ∈ {execution, planning}; execution ⇒ configured SPEC type
- backlog ⇒ source, since, reason (+ age warning); draft_pool ⇒ reason
- closed archives: all items have outcomes; completed ⇒ 100% primary snapshot;
  outcome kinds/reasons as today
- carryover linearity: carried_over in archive N targets archive N+1 (ULID
  order) or, for the last archive, the current active sprint; matching
  carryover item must exist in the target (archive item or live file with
  carryover_from=N)
- stale-reference health for live active items (unchanged)
- `_missing_post_init_specs`: non-terminal SPEC dated ≥ first sprint start must
  have a live file (any scope) or appear in any closed archive

Changed:
- "active sprint item must not have outcome" → live outcome kinds restricted to
  the mid-sprint set; outcome'd items are excluded from live membership
- terminal-SPEC-as-live-item error applies only to outcome-LESS items
- exactly-one-active-sprint: structural (single state.yaml field); validation
  instead asserts no archive has status != closed and no archive id == active id
- duplicate live membership: structural; keep a defensive check that parses all
  live files and errors on document field duplicates (filename mismatch case)

## 8. Migration — `decree migrate sprint-ledger`

1. Require v1 ledger.yaml; require absence of state.yaml.
2. Closed sprints → closed/<id>.yaml (verbatim semantics).
3. Active sprint → state.yaml active {id, name, started}; its (necessarily
   outcome-less) items → live/<doc>.yaml scope=active preserving kind, source,
   added, carryover_from.
4. backlog → live scope=backlog; draft_pool → live scope=draft_pool.
5. Delete ledger.yaml (git history preserves it); print per-file summary.
6. Run v2 validation; non-zero exit on errors.

Then dogfood: run the migration on this repo's own ledger.

## 9. Consumers to update (confirm against recon)

commands/sprint.py (all subcommands), commands/new.py (auto-enroll →
create_live_item), commands/lint.py (validate_ledger signature),
commands/progress.py + ddd.py (select_sprint_scope — API preserved),
commands/generate_html.py (board reads v2), commands/mcp_server.py (if it
exposes sprint tools), cli.py (parsers + help), identity.py (unchanged).

## 10. Tests (new/updated)

- v2 storage round-trip: state/live/closed load-save fidelity
- every operation: init, add×3 scopes, complete (100% gate), drop, pause gate,
  resume, rollover (open vs outcome'd partition, carryover fold)
- migration: full v1 fixture (closed+active+backlog+draft_pool+carryover) → v2
  equivalence; guards (missing v1, existing v2)
- v1-detected error path on every sprint entry point
- validation: each invariant listed in §7, positive + negative
- parallel-simulation test (no git needed): copy a project root twice, create a
  different spec+live file in each copy, union the file sets back together,
  assert lint passes and both docs are in scope; same-doc-two-scopes union →
  lint error (not crash)
- lock: state transition under held lock blocks/errors cleanly (skip on
  platforms without fcntl)
- CLI-level: help output contains new subcommands; exit codes

## 11. Documentation surfaces (confirm against recon)

usage.md (sprint section rewrite: layout, complete, migrate, parallel/worktree
workflow incl. orchestrator patterns), README (if it mentions sprints),
AGENTS.md, root CLAUDE.md quick ref, src/decree/CLAUDE.md commands table,
docs/architecture.md, docs/index.md, docs/plans/ (new dated design note or
banner on the 2026-06-26 one), towncrier fragment, argparse help strings.

## 12. Dogfooding sequence (decree used on decree)

1. `decree new prd "Parallel-safe sprint execution tracking"` (references old PRD)
2. `decree new adr "Directory-decomposed sprint ledger"` → accept → supersedes
   ADR-01KW1QGHBT2BSZ2HVT55G1QSJB (via `decree status ... supersede`)
3. `decree new spec "Sprint ledger v2 storage and item-level completion"` →
   auto-enrolls in active sprint (v1 path, one last time)
4. implement → `decree migrate sprint-ledger` on this repo
5. check off SPEC acceptance criteria as phases land
6. `decree sprint complete SPEC-<new>` + `decree status SPEC-<new> implement`
7. `decree lint` green end-to-end

## 13. Out of scope (explicit, to stay debt-free not scope-creepy)

- Multiple concurrent active sprints / lanes (rejected in ADR)
- Reopen semantics for terminal SPECs (unchanged from v1)
- Cross-branch decree reads (orchestrator merges or runs decree per worktree
  dir; both documented)
- Windows file locking (no-op fallback, documented)

## 13. Out of scope (explicit, to stay debt-free not scope-creepy)

- Multiple concurrent active sprints / lanes (rejected in ADR)
- Reopen semantics for terminal SPECs (unchanged from v1)
- Cross-branch decree reads (orchestrator merges or runs decree per worktree
  dir; both documented)
- Windows file locking (no-op fallback, documented)

python3 - <<'EOF'
import json
data = json.load(open('/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-doruk-Desktop-SIDE-HUSTLE-decree/9aba7607-c379-42b0-82e5-f238fc1a47cb/tasks/w0qkp3ylw.output'))
res = data['result']
for key in ['sprint-consumers', 'tests-inventory', 'docs-inventory', 'dogfood-state']:
    section = res[key]
    print(f"\n{'='*20} {key} {'='*20}")
    print("SUMMARY:", section['summary'])
    for f in section['findings']:
        detail = f['detail'].replace('\n', ' ')

        print(f"- {f['path']} :: {detail}")

==================== sprint-consumers ====================
SUMMARY: The sprints API surface is fully centralized in src/decree/sprints.py; only 6 modules consume it, and 4 of them go exclusively through two stable entry points (validate_ledger, select_sprint_scope) whose signatures can survive v2 unchanged. Direct SprintLedger-shape consumers are commands/sprint.py (11 symbols), commands/new.py (6 symbols), and commands/generate_html.py (load_ledger + iterates ledger.active/sprints/backlog/draft_pool and duck-types item fields via getattr). commands/mcp_server.py never imports sprints — it is insulated behind progress_for_scope. queries.py, health.py, index.py, report.py, index_db.py, hook.py, init.py, status.py, validators.py, parser.py, config.py are confirmed sprint-free. The hardcoded path decree/sprints/ledger.yaml exists in exactly two places: sprints.py:18 (LEDGER_REL_PATH) and cli.py:264 (help text). identity.py already provides sortable SPRINT-ULID ids ideal for closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml ordering. Key v2 pressure points: save_ledger's whole-file atomic write must split into per-file writers; validate_ledger's "active sprint item must not have outcome" invariant (sprints.py:759) must be deleted; _live_membership/_validate_live_membership become structural; carryover linearity and _missing_post_init_specs need a new cross-archive ordering/first-started source; SprintScope doubles as the generic scope container for non-sprint scopes (doc/chain/governs/changed) and must keep its shape. Note reverse dependency: sprints.py imports commands/report.load_report_config for outcome snapshots — `sprint complete` reuses that logic.
- src/decree/sprints.py:18 :: LEDGER_REL_PATH = Path('decree/sprints/ledger.yaml') — the single hardcoded v1 path constant, plus SCHEMA='decree.sprints.v1' (line 19). v2 replaces these with STATE_REL_PATH (decree/sprints/state.yaml), LIVE_DIR, CLOSED_DIR, and a v2 schema string. sprint_mode_enabled (272) checks ledger_path().exists() — must become state.yaml existence so a leftover v1 file doesn't half-enable mode; lint should error 'run decree migrate sprint-ledger' when ledger.yaml exists.
- src/decree/sprints.py:276-299 :: load_ledger parses one YAML file and checks schema/mode; save_ledger (292) does a whole-ledger atomic tempfile rewrite in the ledger's parent dir. v2 must decompose into load_state/save_state, load_live_items/save_live_item(doc_id) (write ONLY that item's file — required by `decree sprint complete`), load_closed_sprint/save_closed_sprint, plus a composed loader for consumers needing the full picture (generate_html, validate). - src/decree/sprints.py:568-641 :: validate_ledger(root, docs) -> SprintValidation is the lint entry point; keep this signature. The new `decree sprint complete DOC-ID` must reuse exactly this outcome construction + snapshot + 100%-primary gate for kind=completed, writing only live/<DOC-ID>.yaml. Rollover in v2 must then treat already-outcome'd live items a …[cut]
- src/decree/sprints.py:32-243 :: Data model: SprintItem (32: document/kind/source/added/carryover_from/outcome), BacklogItem (65: +since/review_after/reason), DraftPoolItem (102: document/kind/added/reason), SprintRecord (128: id/name/status/started/closed/items), SprintLedger (163: schema/mode/state/active/paused/sprints/backlog/draft_pool, active_sprint property 224). v2 likely unifies the three item classes into one LiveItem with a scope field (active|backlog|draft_pool) matching the per-file schema; keep attribute names doc …[cut]

- src/decree/commands/new.py:13-20 :: Imports SprintLedgerError, add_to_active_sprint, add_to_backlog, add_to_draft_pool, load_ledger, sprint_mode_enabled. SUMMARY: Sprint test surface is small and self-contained: 3 files touch sprint/ledger. tests/test_sprint.py (14 tests) is the core; tests/test_generate_html.py (3 tests) and one help-test in tests/test_cli.py are adjacent. No parametrization anywhere in sprint tests. Every ledger fixture hand-writes the v1 monolithic ledger.yaml as an inline f-string heredoc into decree/sprints/ (8 occurrences total: 7 in test_sprint.py, 1 in test_generate_html.py) — all must be rewritten for v2's state.yaml + live/ + closed/ layout, ideally via one new shared helper. aiTitle: Ledger locking issue in parallel development

input.content:
# Sprint Ledger v2 — Design Document (FINAL — single source of truth)

Repo: /Users/doruk/Desktop/SIDE_HUSTLE/decree
Status: FINAL. Implementation agents follow this exactly; deviations must be reported back, not improvised.

## 1. Problem (context)

`decree/sprints/ledger.yaml` is the only hand-maintained, global, monolithic mutable
file in a system otherwise built on one-file-per-decision documents and derived
indexes. It serializes parallel work: (1) `decree new spec` auto-enrolls into the
active sprint → every worktree appends to the same YAML list → merge conflicts;
(2) lint's enrollment invariant forces SPEC file + ledger entry into the same
commit; (3) exactly-one-active-sprint + rollover-requires-all-outcomes couples
parallel streams; (4) load→mutate→save has no lock → same-worktree sessions lose
writes. Also item-level completion is inexpressible mid-sprint (sprints.py:759
forbids outcomes on active items; sprints.py:776 forbids terminal live SPECs).

Principle: do not relax invariants; shrink the contention surface. One file per
unit of change; semantic conflicts surface in post-merge lint.

## 2. v2 storage layout (schema `decree.sprints.v2`)

```
decree/sprints/
  state.yaml                # changes ONLY at init/pause/resume/rollover
  live/<DOC-ID>.yaml        # one file per live membership (uppercase doc id + .yaml)
  closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml   # one append-only archive per closed sprint
```

### state.yaml
```yaml
schema: decree.sprints.v2
mode: enabled
state: active            # or: paused
active:                  # present iff state == active
  id: SPRINT-<ULID>
  name: Sprint 2 - ...
  started: '2026-06-26'
paused:                  # present iff state == paused
  since: '2026-07-03'
  reason: summer freeze
```

### live/<DOC-ID>.yaml
```yaml
document: SPEC-<ULID>     # MUST equal filename stem (uppercase)
scope: active             # active | backlog | draft_pool
kind: execution           # execution | planning
source: new               # manual | new | carryover | deferred
added: '2026-07-03'
since: '2026-07-03'       # backlog only
reason: ...               # required for backlog and draft_pool
review_after: '2026-08-01'  # optional, backlog only
carryover_from: SPRINT-<ULID>  # optional; scope=active carryover items only
outcome:                  # optional; PRESENT ⇒ item no longer counts as live membership
  kind: completed         # mid-sprint set: completed | dropped ONLY
  at: '2026-07-03'
  reason: ...             # required for dropped
  evidence: {commits: [...]}
  snapshot: {status, primary_done, primary_total, deferred_done, deferred_total}
```

KEY SEMANTIC: live scope=active items do NOT record a sprint id. A live item
belongs to whichever sprint is active at integration time; sprint attribution is
written only when a sprint closes (fold into closed/). carried_over, deferred,
superseded remain ROLLOVER-ONLY outcome kinds (they fold/relocate membership);
completed and dropped are additionally available mid-sprint via new commands.

### closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml
Exactly today's closed SprintRecord shape: id, name, status: closed, started,
closed, items[] each with mandatory outcome (kind/at/reason/to_sprint/
to_document/evidence/snapshot) and carryover_from where applicable.
Chronological order of sprints = ULID-lexicographic order of filenames.

## 3. In-memory model (sprints.py rewrite)

- `SprintState` frozen dataclass ← state.yaml (schema, mode, state, active:{id,name,started}|None, paused|None)
- `LiveItem` frozen dataclass ← live/*.yaml (unifies v1 SprintItem/BacklogItem/DraftPoolItem; fields per schema above)
- `SprintRecord`/`SprintItem` KEPT for closed archives (same fields as v1)
- `LedgerView`: {state, live: dict[doc_id → LiveItem], closed: tuple[SprintRecord,...]}
  helpers: active_items (all scope=active), active_open_items (outcome is None),
  active_done_items, backlog_items, draft_pool_items, live_membership() → dict
  doc→scope counting ONLY outcome-less files.

I/O functions (writes touch ONLY the affected file):
- `load_state(root)` — cheap read of state.yaml only (used by new.py gating)
- `load_view(root)` — assemble full directory
- `save_state(state, root)` — tempfile+rename, under `_ledger_lock(root)`
- `create_live_item(item, root)` — os.open(O_CREAT|O_EXCL|O_WRONLY) for atomic
  exists-check; SprintLedgerError if the doc already has ANY live file (message
  distinguishes outcome'd files: "resolved record folds at next rollover")
- `rewrite_live_item(item, root)` — tempfile+rename (used by complete/drop)
- `remove_live_item(doc_id, root)` — fold-time only (rollover/pause)
- `write_closed_sprint(record, root)` — new archive file, error if exists
- `_ledger_lock(root)` — contextmanager, fcntl.flock EX on decree/sprints/.lock;
  on ImportError (no fcntl) yield without locking (documented no-op). Used by
  init/pause/resume/rollover only.

v1 handling: `LEDGER_REL_PATH` (v1 ledger.yaml) kept ONLY for detection + the
migrate command's private reader. Every sprint entry point (load_state,
load_view, sprint_mode_enabled callers) raises SprintLedgerError
"sprint ledger v1 detected; run `decree migrate sprint-ledger`" when ledger.yaml
exists and state.yaml does not. `sprint_mode_enabled(root)` returns True if
state.yaml OR legacy ledger.yaml exists (so lint surfaces the migration error
loudly instead of silently skipping sprint checks). validate_ledger returns that
error as its single error in that case.

## 4. Operations (public API, consumed by commands/sprint.py, new.py)

- `init_ledger(name, root, today)` — mkdir sprints/live+closed, write state.yaml.
  Errors if state.yaml OR v1 ledger.yaml exists.
- `add_to_active_sprint(document, kind, source, root, today)` — requires state
  active; create_live_item(scope=active). Structural duplicate check.
- `add_to_backlog(document, reason, kind, source, root, today, review_after)` /
  `add_to_draft_pool(...)` — create_live_item with scope + reason (+since for
  backlog). Allowed in paused state (v1 parity).
- `complete_item(document, commits, root, today)` NEW — item must be a live
  scope=active outcome-less item; snapshot via _snapshot_for_doc; gate: primary
  ACs 100% (reuse exact message "cannot be completed unless primary acceptance
  criteria are 100%"); write outcome {kind: completed, at, evidence:{commits},
  snapshot} via rewrite_live_item. Works for execution and planning items.
- `drop_item(document, reason, root, today)` NEW — same mechanics, kind=dropped,
  reason required, snapshot recorded for audit.
- `pause_ledger(reason, root, today)` — requires state active; all scope=active
  items must HAVE outcomes (open items → error "cannot pause with open
  active-sprint items; complete, drop, or rollover them first"); fold outcome'd
  active items into closed/<active-id>.yaml (status closed, closed=today),
  remove their live files, state→paused. Under lock.
- `resume_ledger(name, root, today)` — paused→active with fresh SPRINT-ULID.
- `rollover_ledger(name, outcomes, docs, root, today)` — outcomes file must
  cover EXACTLY the OPEN active items (outcome'd ones already resolved; error
  messages for missing/extra keep v1 wording). Build closed record from state
  .active + all active items (pre-outcome'd + newly outcome'd); completed gate
  100%; write archive; remove folded live files; create carryover live files
  (scope=active, source=carryover, carryover_from=old id, added=today); update
  state.active. Under lock. carried_over outcome.to_sprint = new sprint id.
- `load_outcomes_file(path)` — unchanged.
- `select_sprint_scope(docs, args, root)` — SIGNATURE + SprintScope shape
  unchanged (generic scope container used by progress/ddd/mcp). Sources:
  default → live scope=active outcome-less; --sprint ID → active id ⇒ ALL
  active items incl. outcome'd, else closed archive items; --all-sprints →
  archives + active items; --backlog/--draft-pool → live by scope; paused
  default → same error text as v1.
- `validate_ledger(root, docs)` — SIGNATURE unchanged; see §5.
- `sprint_mode_enabled(root)` — see §3.

## 5. Validation (validate_ledger v2)

Kept, rebased onto layout: schema/mode; active⇒active block complete; paused⇒
since+reason; paused ⇒ no scope=active live files; live filename stem ==
document field (uppercase) + canonical doc id + document exists in corpus +
kind valid + execution⇒SPEC type; backlog⇒source/since/reason + age warning
(BACKLOG_WARN_AFTER_DAYS=30 stays hardcoded); draft_pool⇒reason; closed
archives: status closed + closed date + every item has outcome + outcome
validation incl. completed⇒100% snapshot (keep message "completed outcome
requires snapshot primary progress at 100%"); carryover linearity across
ULID-ordered archives, last archive may target the CURRENT active sprint id
(matching live file with carryover_from required); stale-reference health for
live active outcome-less items (keep message); _missing_post_init_specs: keep
message "must be in active sprint, backlog, or draft_pool"; a doc satisfies
enrollment if it has ANY live file (any scope, outcome'd or not) or appears in
any closed archive; first_started = min(started of archives + active).

Changed:
- "active sprint item must not have outcome" DELETED. Live outcome kinds
  restricted to {completed, dropped}; other kinds in a live file = error.
- outcome only legal on scope=active live files.
- terminal-SPEC-as-live-item error only for outcome-LESS items (v1 already
  gates on outcome is None — keep).
- one-active-sprint: structural; validate no archive has status != closed and
  no archive id == state.active.id and no duplicate archive ids.
- duplicate live membership: structural; keep defensive error if two live files
  declare the same document field (filename mismatch case).
- new: malformed YAML / non-mapping live or closed files → per-file error, not crash.

## 6. CLI changes (cli.py + commands/)

- sprint parser description (cli.py:264): rewrite — directory store
  (decree/sprints/: state.yaml + live/ + closed/), init creates state.yaml.
- NEW `sprint complete DOCUMENT [--commit SHA]...` (--commit repeatable,
  evidence commits; help: record a completed outcome for one item mid-sprint;
  requires 100% primary acceptance criteria).
- NEW `sprint drop DOCUMENT --reason TEXT` (mid-sprint dropped outcome).
- `sprint pause` help: "...after every active item is completed, dropped, or
  rolled over".
- `sprint rollover --outcomes` help: "...mapping each OPEN active sprint
  document (items completed or dropped mid-sprint are already resolved)...".
- `sprint status` output: state + active sprint id/name + open items + done
  items ("done (awaiting rollover)") + backlog + draft pool counts.
- NEW `migrate sprint-ledger` parser: required mutually-exclusive --dry-run |
  --apply, plus --project PATH (default cwd). NO --json (matches migrate ids).
  Wire in _migrate_dispatch (cli.py:1150) → migrate.py:migrate_sprint_ledger_run.
  Update migrate namespace description (cli.py:916-925) to enumerate it.
- commands/sprint.py run(): add complete/drop branches; port all existing
  branches to the new API; keep draft-pool/draft alias handling.
- commands/new.py: gating reads load_state (cheap); enrollment writes one live
  file via the §4 API; error text unchanged.
- commands/generate_html.py: build board from load_view — synthesize active
  SprintRecord (id/name/started from state, items from live active as
  SprintItems incl. outcomes), closed records from archives ULID-sorted, active
  last; ledger.backlog/draft_pool ← live files. Board payload decree.board.v1
  UNCHANGED. Default selected sprint = active (fallback: last closed).
- commands/migrate.py: migrate_sprint_ledger_run(args) — see §7.
- commands/mcp_server.py: NO code change (insulated via progress_for_scope).

## 7. Migration — `decree migrate sprint-ledger`

Private v1 reader (move v1 from_raw parsing into migrate.py or a _v1 module-
private section of sprints.py). Plan: closed sprints → closed/<id>.yaml;
active sprint → state.yaml active{id,name,started} + its items (v1 guarantees
outcome-less) → live/<doc>.yaml scope=active preserving kind/source/added/
carryover_from; backlog → scope=backlog (since/review_after/reason/source);
draft_pool → scope=draft_pool. --dry-run prints the plan (files to create,
ledger.yaml removal) to stdout, writes nothing, exit 0. --apply: guard state
.yaml absent + ledger.yaml present (else exit 2 with message); write all files;
delete ledger.yaml; print summary; run validate_ledger and exit 1 on errors,
0 clean. Follow migrate ids' plan→apply structure (migrate.py:694-729).

## 8. Tests (conventions are binding)

Conventions: module-level helpers (_write_config/_write_doc), all-zero ULIDs,
monkeypatch.chdir(tmp_path), in-process <module>.run(argparse.Namespace(...))
with EVERY flag defaulted, capsys+json.loads, assert exit codes 0/1, model
inspection via load_view/validate_ledger; error-message substrings are
load-bearing. conftest autouse reset_caches exists. No parametrize convention.

Rewrite 8 inline v1 ledger fixtures (7 in test_sprint.py, 1 in
test_generate_html.py) to v2 via ONE shared helper (e.g. _write_v2_ledger(root,
state=..., live=[...], closed=[...])). New tests:
- storage round-trip + filename↔document mismatch error + malformed live file error
- init (creates state.yaml + dirs; errors on existing state.yaml / v1 ledger)
- v1-detected error from lint + sprint status + new spec (exit 1, migrate hint)
- complete: happy path (outcome+snapshot in own file only), <100% gate refusal,
  planning item, already-outcome'd refusal, not-in-active refusal; after
  complete: doc → terminal status keeps lint green; default progress scope
  excludes it; --sprint <active> includes it; snapshot immutability (uncheck a
  box after complete — snapshot stable)
- drop: reason required; outcome recorded
- pause: refuses with open items (new message); folds done items into archive
- rollover: outcomes for open items only; pre-completed item folds with its
  mid-sprint outcome; carryover creates live file with carryover_from; archive
  written; state updated
- migrate: dogfood-shaped fixture (closed sprint w/ carried_over+completed
  incl. snapshots, active w/ carryover item, backlog, draft_pool) → apply →
  load_view equivalence + validate_ledger clean + ledger.yaml gone; --dry-run
  writes nothing; guards (no v1 file; v2 already present) exit 2
- validation: each §5 invariant positive+negative (update duplicate-membership
  test to the structural reality: two live files same document field)
- parallel-simulation (no git): project A copied to B; create different
  spec+live in each; union files back; lint green + both in scope. Same doc in
  two copies with different scopes → after union, structural duplicate =
  the-one-file conflict cannot be represented → simulate two live files w/
  mismatched names → lint error not crash
- lock: init/rollover acquire .lock (fcntl present ⇒ flock called; skip test if
  no fcntl)
- CLI help smoke (test_cli.py subprocess style): `sprint complete --help`,
  `sprint drop --help`, `migrate sprint-ledger --help`
- keep CLI/MCP parity + generate-html board tests passing (fixture → v2)
Update tests/CLAUDE.md test map (add sprint/generate_html/migrate rows).

## 9. Documentation surfaces (all binding)

- docs/usage.md: sprint section rewrite (directory store, complete, drop, new
  pause/rollover semantics, parallel-worktree workflow + orchestrator patterns:
  per-worktree inspection & integration-merge lint); new `### decree migrate
  sprint-ledger` section; progress/ddd notes (outcome'd items leave default
  scope); generate-html data-source wording.
- docs/index.md: capability rows (37-40) + onboarding step 4/5 (state.yaml,
  sprint complete, migrate sprint-ledger).
- docs/architecture.md: add sprints.py module row (v2 directory store; second
  on-disk store distinct from parser.py's document-file rule) + commands.
- src/decree/CLAUDE.md: module row for sprints.py + command rows for sprint
  (incl. complete/drop) and migrate (incl. sprint-ledger).
- src/decree/commands/AGENTS.md: sprint.py family entry + extend migrate.py line.
- tests/CLAUDE.md: test map rows.
- Root CLAUDE.md: quick-ref lines (sprint init/complete, migrate sprint-ledger)
  + Key Design Decisions bullet (outcome ≠ live; one-active-sprint structural;
  live items sprint-agnostic until fold).
- AGENTS.md: short sprint-mode note (default scoping, sprint complete when ACs
  done, --corpus escape hatch).
- skills/decree-ddd/SKILL.md AND byte-identical
  src/decree/templates/agent/skills/decree-ddd/SKILL.md (must stay identical):
  add work-loop step — when a SPEC's primary ACs are done mid-sprint, run
  `decree sprint complete DOC-ID`.
- docs/plans/: new dated note 2026-07-03-parallel-safe-sprint-ledger.md

toolUseResult.content:
# Sprint Ledger v2 — Design Document (FINAL — single source of truth)

Repo: /Users/doruk/Desktop/SIDE_HUSTLE/decree
Status: FINAL. Implementation agents follow this exactly; deviations must be reported back, not improvised.

## 1. Problem (context)

`decree/sprints/ledger.yaml` is the only hand-maintained, global, monolithic mutable
file in a system otherwise built on one-file-per-decision documents and derived
indexes. It serializes parallel work: (1) `decree new spec` auto-enrolls into the
active sprint → every worktree appends to the same YAML list → merge conflicts;
(2) lint's enrollment invariant forces SPEC file + ledger entry into the same
commit; (3) exactly-one-active-sprint + rollover-requires-all-outcomes couples
parallel streams; (4) load→mutate→save has no lock → same-worktree sessions lose
writes. Also item-level completion is inexpressible mid-sprint (sprints.py:759
forbids outcomes on active items; sprints.py:776 forbids terminal live SPECs).

Principle: do not relax invariants; shrink the contention surface. One file per
unit of change; semantic conflicts surface in post-merge lint.

## 2. v2 storage layout (schema `decree.sprints.v2`)

```
decree/sprints/
  state.yaml                # changes ONLY at init/pause/resume/rollover
  live/<DOC-ID>.yaml        # one file per live membership (uppercase doc id + .yaml)
  closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml   # one append-only archive per closed sprint
```

### state.yaml
```yaml
schema: decree.sprints.v2
mode: enabled
state: active            # or: paused
active:                  # present iff state == active
  id: SPRINT-<ULID>
  name: Sprint 2 - ...
  started: '2026-06-26'
paused:                  # present iff state == paused
  since: '2026-07-03'
  reason: summer freeze
```

### live/<DOC-ID>.yaml
```yaml
document: SPEC-<ULID>     # MUST equal filename stem (uppercase)
scope: active             # active | backlog | draft_pool
kind: execution           # execution | planning
source: new               # manual | new | carryover | deferred
added: '2026-07-03'
since: '2026-07-03'       # backlog only
reason: ...               # required for backlog and draft_pool
review_after: '2026-08-01'  # optional, backlog only
carryover_from: SPRINT-<ULID>  # optional; scope=active carryover items only
outcome:                  # optional; PRESENT ⇒ item no longer counts as live membership
  kind: completed         # mid-sprint set: completed | dropped ONLY
  at: '2026-07-03'
  reason: ...             # required for dropped
  evidence: {commits: [...]}
  snapshot: {status, primary_done, primary_total, deferred_done, deferred_total}
```

KEY SEMANTIC: live scope=active items do NOT record a sprint id. A live item
belongs to whichever sprint is active at integration time; sprint attribution is
written only when a sprint closes (fold into closed/). carried_over, deferred,
superseded remain ROLLOVER-ONLY outcome kinds (they fold/relocate membership);
completed and dropped are additionally available mid-sprint via new commands.

### closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml
Exactly today's closed SprintRecord shape: id, name, status: closed, started,
closed, items[] each with mandatory outcome (kind/at/reason/to_sprint/
to_document/evidence/snapshot) and carryover_from where applicable.
Chronological order of sprints = ULID-lexicographic order of filenames.

## 3. In-memory model (sprints.py rewrite)

- `SprintState` frozen dataclass ← state.yaml (schema, mode, state, active:{id,name,started}|None, paused|None)
- `LiveItem` frozen dataclass ← live/*.yaml (unifies v1 SprintItem/BacklogItem/DraftPoolItem; fields per schema above)
- `SprintRecord`/`SprintItem` KEPT for closed archives (same fields as v1)
- `LedgerView`: {state, live: dict[doc_id → LiveItem], closed: tuple[SprintRecord,...]}
  helpers: active_items (all scope=active), active_open_items (outcome is None),
  active_done_items, backlog_items, draft_pool_items, live_membership() → dict
  doc→scope counting ONLY outcome-less files.

I/O functions (writes touch ONLY the affected file):
- `load_state(root)` — cheap read of state.yaml only (used by new.py gating)
- `load_view(root)` — assemble full directory
- `save_state(state, root)` — tempfile+rename, under `_ledger_lock(root)`
- `create_live_item(item, root)` — os.open(O_CREAT|O_EXCL|O_WRONLY) for atomic
  exists-check; SprintLedgerError if the doc already has ANY live file (message
  distinguishes outcome'd files: "resolved record folds at next rollover")
- `rewrite_live_item(item, root)` — tempfile+rename (used by complete/drop)
- `remove_live_item(doc_id, root)` — fold-time only (rollover/pause)
- `write_closed_sprint(record, root)` — new archive file, error if exists
- `_ledger_lock(root)` — contextmanager, fcntl.flock EX on decree/sprints/.lock;
  on ImportError (no fcntl) yield without locking (documented no-op). Used by
  init/pause/resume/rollover only.

v1 handling: `LEDGER_REL_PATH` (v1 ledger.yaml) kept ONLY for detection + the
migrate command's private reader. Every sprint entry point (load_state,
load_view, sprint_mode_enabled callers) raises SprintLedgerError
"sprint ledger v1 detected; run `decree migrate sprint-ledger`" when ledger.yaml
exists and state.yaml does not. `sprint_mode_enabled(root)` returns True if
state.yaml OR legacy ledger.yaml exists (so lint surfaces the migration error
loudly instead of silently skipping sprint checks). validate_ledger returns that
error as its single error in that case.

## 4. Operations (public API, consumed by commands/sprint.py, new.py)

- `init_ledger(name, root, today)` — mkdir sprints/live+closed, write state.yaml.
  Errors if state.yaml OR v1 ledger.yaml exists.
- `add_to_active_sprint(document, kind, source, root, today)` — requires state
  active; create_live_item(scope=active). Structural duplicate check.
- `add_to_backlog(document, reason, kind, source, root, today, review_after)` /
  `add_to_draft_pool(...)` — create_live_item with scope + reason (+since for
  backlog). Allowed in paused state (v1 parity).
- `complete_item(document, commits, root, today)` NEW — item must be a live
  scope=active outcome-less item; snapshot via _snapshot_for_doc; gate: primary
  ACs 100% (reuse exact message "cannot be completed unless primary acceptance
  criteria are 100%"); write outcome {kind: completed, at, evidence:{commits},
  snapshot} via rewrite_live_item. Works for execution and planning items.
- `drop_item(document, reason, root, today)` NEW — same mechanics, kind=dropped,
  reason required, snapshot recorded for audit.
- `pause_ledger(reason, root, today)` — requires state active; all scope=active
  items must HAVE outcomes (open items → error "cannot pause with open
  active-sprint items; complete, drop, or rollover them first"); fold outcome'd
  active items into closed/<active-id>.yaml (status closed, closed=today),
  remove their live files, state→paused. Under lock.
- `resume_ledger(name, root, today)` — paused→active with fresh SPRINT-ULID.
- `rollover_ledger(name, outcomes, docs, root, today)` — outcomes file must
  cover EXACTLY the OPEN active items (outcome'd ones already resolved; error
  messages for missing/extra keep v1 wording). Build closed record from state
  .active + all active items (pre-outcome'd + newly outcome'd); completed gate
  100%; write archive; remove folded live files; create carryover live files
  (scope=active, source=carryover, carryover_from=old id, added=today); update
  state.active. Under lock. carried_over outcome.to_sprint = new sprint id.
- `load_outcomes_file(path)` — unchanged.
- `select_sprint_scope(docs, args, root)` — SIGNATURE + SprintScope shape
  unchanged (generic scope container used by progress/ddd/mcp). Sources:
  default → live scope=active outcome-less; --sprint ID → active id ⇒ ALL
  active items incl. outcome'd, else closed archive items; --all-sprints →
  archives + active items; --backlog/--draft-pool → live by scope; paused
  default → same error text as v1.
- `validate_ledger(root, docs)` — SIGNATURE unchanged; see §5.
- `sprint_mode_enabled(root)` — see §3.

## 5. Validation (validate_ledger v2)

Kept, rebased onto layout: schema/mode; active⇒active block complete; paused⇒
since+reason; paused ⇒ no scope=active live files; live filename stem ==
document field (uppercase) + canonical doc id + document exists in corpus +
kind valid + execution⇒SPEC type; backlog⇒source/since/reason + age warning
(BACKLOG_WARN_AFTER_DAYS=30 stays hardcoded); draft_pool⇒reason; closed
archives: status closed + closed date + every item has outcome + outcome
validation incl. completed⇒100% snapshot (keep message "completed outcome
requires snapshot primary progress at 100%"); carryover linearity across
ULID-ordered archives, last archive may target the CURRENT active sprint id
(matching live file with carryover_from required); stale-reference health for
live active outcome-less items (keep message); _missing_post_init_specs: keep
message "must be in active sprint, backlog, or draft_pool"; a doc satisfies
enrollment if it has ANY live file (any scope, outcome'd or not) or appears in
any closed archive; first_started = min(started of archives + active).

Changed:
- "active sprint item must not have outcome" DELETED. Live outcome kinds
  restricted to {completed, dropped}; other kinds in a live file = error.
- outcome only legal on scope=active live files.
- terminal-SPEC-as-live-item error only for outcome-LESS items (v1 already
  gates on outcome is None — keep).
- one-active-sprint: structural; validate no archive has status != closed and
  no archive id == state.active.id and no duplicate archive ids.
- duplicate live membership: structural; keep defensive error if two live files
  declare the same document field (filename mismatch case).
- new: malformed YAML / non-mapping live or closed files → per-file error, not crash.

## 6. CLI changes (cli.py + commands/)

- sprint parser description (cli.py:264): rewrite — directory store
  (decree/sprints/: state.yaml + live/ + closed/), init creates state.yaml.
- NEW `sprint complete DOCUMENT [--commit SHA]...` (--commit repeatable,
  evidence commits; help: record a completed outcome for one item mid-sprint;
  requires 100% primary acceptance criteria).
- NEW `sprint drop DOCUMENT --reason TEXT` (mid-sprint dropped outcome).
- `sprint pause` help: "...after every active item is completed, dropped, or
  rolled over".
- `sprint rollover --outcomes` help: "...mapping each OPEN active sprint
  document (items completed or dropped mid-sprint are already resolved)...".
- `sprint status` output: state + active sprint id/name + open items + done
  items ("done (awaiting rollover)") + backlog + draft pool counts.
- NEW `migrate sprint-ledger` parser: required mutually-exclusive --dry-run |
  --apply, plus --project PATH (default cwd). NO --json (matches migrate ids).
  Wire in _migrate_dispatch (cli.py:1150) → migrate.py:migrate_sprint_ledger_run.
  Update migrate namespace description (cli.py:916-925) to enumerate it.
- commands/sprint.py run(): add complete/drop branches; port all existing
  branches to the new API; keep draft-pool/draft alias handling.
- commands/new.py: gating reads load_state (cheap); enrollment writes one live
  file via the §4 API; error text unchanged.
- commands/generate_html.py: build board from load_view — synthesize active
  SprintRecord (id/name/started from state, items from live active as
  SprintItems incl. outcomes), closed records from archives ULID-sorted, active
  last; ledger.backlog/draft_pool ← live files. Board payload decree.board.v1
  UNCHANGED. Default selected sprint = active (fallback: last closed).
- commands/migrate.py: migrate_sprint_ledger_run(args) — see §7.
- commands/mcp_server.py: NO code change (insulated via progress_for_scope).

## 7. Migration — `decree migrate sprint-ledger`

Private v1 reader (move v1 from_raw parsing into migrate.py or a _v1 module-
private section of sprints.py). Plan: closed sprints → closed/<id>.yaml;
active sprint → state.yaml active{id,name,started} + its items (v1 guarantees
outcome-less) → live/<doc>.yaml scope=active preserving kind/source/added/
carryover_from; backlog → scope=backlog (since/review_after/reason/source);
draft_pool → scope=draft_pool. --dry-run prints the plan (files to create,
ledger.yaml removal) to stdout, writes nothing, exit 0. --apply: guard state
.yaml absent + ledger.yaml present (else exit 2 with message); write all files;
delete ledger.yaml; print summary; run validate_ledger and exit 1 on errors,
0 clean. Follow migrate ids' plan→apply structure (migrate.py:694-729).

## 8. Tests (conventions are binding)

Conventions: module-level helpers (_write_config/_write_doc), all-zero ULIDs,
monkeypatch.chdir(tmp_path), in-process <module>.run(argparse.Namespace(...))
with EVERY flag defaulted, capsys+json.loads, assert exit codes 0/1, model
inspection via load_view/validate_ledger; error-message substrings are
load-bearing. conftest autouse reset_caches exists. No parametrize convention.

Rewrite 8 inline v1 ledger fixtures (7 in test_sprint.py, 1 in
test_generate_html.py) to v2 via ONE shared helper (e.g. _write_v2_ledger(root,
state=..., live=[...], closed=[...])). New tests:
- storage round-trip + filename↔document mismatch error + malformed live file error
- init (creates state.yaml + dirs; errors on existing state.yaml / v1 ledger)
- v1-detected error from lint + sprint status + new spec (exit 1, migrate hint)
- complete: happy path (outcome+snapshot in own file only), <100% gate refusal,
  planning item, already-outcome'd refusal, not-in-active refusal; after
  complete: doc → terminal status keeps lint green; default progress scope
  excludes it; --sprint <active> includes it; snapshot immutability (uncheck a
  box after complete — snapshot stable)
- drop: reason required; outcome recorded
- pause: refuses with open items (new message); folds done items into archive
- rollover: outcomes for open items only; pre-completed item folds with its
  mid-sprint outcome; carryover creates live file with carryover_from; archive
  written; state updated
- migrate: dogfood-shaped fixture (closed sprint w/ carried_over+completed
  incl. snapshots, active w/ carryover item, backlog, draft_pool) → apply →
  load_view equivalence + validate_ledger clean + ledger.yaml gone; --dry-run
  writes nothing; guards (no v1 file; v2 already present) exit 2
- validation: each §5 invariant positive+negative (update duplicate-membership
  test to the structural reality: two live files same document field)
- parallel-simulation (no git): project A copied to B; create different
  spec+live in each; union files back; lint green + both in scope. Same doc in
  two copies with different scopes → after union, structural duplicate =
  the-one-file conflict cannot be represented → simulate two live files w/
  mismatched names → lint error not crash
- lock: init/rollover acquire .lock (fcntl present ⇒ flock called; skip test if
  no fcntl)
- CLI help smoke (test_cli.py subprocess style): `sprint complete --help`,
  `sprint drop --help`, `migrate sprint-ledger --help`
- keep CLI/MCP parity + generate-html board tests passing (fixture → v2)
Update tests/CLAUDE.md test map (add sprint/generate_html/migrate rows).

## 9. Documentation surfaces (all binding)

- docs/usage.md: sprint section rewrite (directory store, complete, drop, new
  pause/rollover semantics, parallel-worktree workflow + orchestrator patterns:
  per-worktree inspection & integration-merge lint); new `### decree migrate
  sprint-ledger` section; progress/ddd notes (outcome'd items leave default

- src/decree/CLAUDE.md: module row for sprints.py + command rows for sprint

input.description: Dogfood PRD/ADR/SPEC via decree
input.prompt:
You are working in /Users/doruk/Desktop/SIDE_HUSTLE/decree (a Python CLI called decree that manages PRD/ADR/SPEC decision documents; it dogfoods itself in decree/).

FIRST read the design document: /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-doruk-Desktop-SIDE-HUSTLE-decree/9aba7607-c379-42b0-82e5-f238fc1a47cb/scratchpad/sprint-v2-design.md — especially §1, §2, §10 (dogfooding sequence Phase 2). Your job is EXACTLY §10 Phase 2: create the dogfood decision documents for the sprint-ledger v2 redesign USING THE DECREE CLI, before any code changes exist. Do NOT modify anything under src/ or tests/.

Steps (run from repo root; all commands `uv run decree ...`):
1. Read for style/structure reference: decree/prd/prd-01kw1qezec1r1bstzn1ssppcej-sprint-scoped-execution-tracking.md, decree/adr/REDACTED.md, decree/spec/REDACTED.md. Also read decree.toml to learn required sections per type and the exact status transition/action names.
2. `uv run decree new prd "Parallel-Safe Sprint Execution" --bucket sprints` — note the created path/ID from stdout.
3. `uv run decree new adr "Directory-Decomposed Sprint Ledger" --bucket sprints`
4. `uv run decree new spec "Sprint Ledger v2 Storage and Item-Level Completion" --bucket sprints` — this will auto-enroll the SPEC in the active sprint via the v1 ledger; that is expected and correct.
5. Fill the three documents with real content derived from the design doc (do not copy it verbatim; write proper document prose):
   - PRD: problem = the monolithic ledger serializes parallel worktree development (the 4 mechanisms in design §1); requirements = conflict-free membership writes across worktrees and concurrent sessions, item-level mid-sprint completion, orchestrator supervision (per-worktree inspection + integration-merge lint), one-shot v1→v2 migration; success criteria as `- [ ]` checkboxes (user-observable outcomes, e.g. "two worktrees each create a SPEC and merge without touching a shared file"). Frontmatter references: [PRD-01KW1QEZEC1R1BSTZN1SSPPCEJ] (hand-edit frontmatter; there is no CLI flag).
   - ADR: Context and Problem Statement; Decision Drivers; Considered Options MUST compare: (a) keep monolith + git merge driver/union, (b) membership in document frontmatter with derived ledger, (c) multiple active sprints/lanes per worktree, (d) directory-decomposed store (state.yaml + live/<DOC-ID>.yaml + closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml) — chosen; Decision Outcome with consequences including: outcome-bearing items are not live membership; live active items are sprint-agnostic until fold (belong to whichever sprint is active at integration); completed/dropped become mid-sprint item-level outcomes; carried_over/deferred/superseded stay rollover-only; rollover remains a deliberately single-writer integration ceremony. references: [the new PRD id, PRD-01KW1QEZEC1R1BSTZN1SSPPCEJ].
   - SPEC: technical spec of design §2-§7: storage layout with YAML examples, in-memory model + per-file write API, operation semantics (init/add/backlog/draft-pool/complete/drop/pause/resume/rollover), validation invariant list (kept vs changed), CLI additions (`sprint complete`, `sprint drop`, `migrate sprint-ledger` with --dry-run|--apply and --project), migration algorithm. Acceptance criteria as `- [ ]` checkboxes covering: v2 store read/write, v1 detection error pointing at migrate command, sprint complete 100% gate + own-file-only write, sprint drop, pause folds resolved items, rollover covers only open items + creates carryover live files + archive, migration of this repo's own ledger, parallel union simulation test green, all docs+help updated. Add a small "Deferred" section for out-of-scope items (design §11). Frontmatter references: [new PRD id, new ADR id]. Frontmatter governs: [src/decree/sprints.py, src/decree/cli.py, src/decree/commands/sprint.py, src/decree/commands/new.py, src/decree/commands/migrate.py, src/decree/commands/generate_html.py, tests/test_sprint.py] (all these paths exist; governs paths must exist on disk or lint fails).
6. `uv run decree status <NEW-ADR-ID> accept`
7. `uv run decree status ADR-01KW1QGHBT2BSZ2HVT55G1QSJB accept` (it is 'proposed'; superseded is only reachable from accepted)
8. `uv run decree status ADR-01KW1QGHBT2BSZ2HVT55G1QSJB supersede <NEW-ADR-ID>` (positional target; writes bidirectional links)
9. Edit decree/spec/REDACTED.md FRONTMATTER ONLY: in its references list replace ADR-01KW1QGHBT2BSZ2HVT55G1QSJB with <NEW-ADR-ID> (references to superseded docs are hard lint errors, doubly so for active sprint items). Do not touch its body.
10. `uv run decree index regenerate`, then `uv run decree lint` — MUST exit 0. If lint reports errors, fix them (missing required sections, reference issues) and re-run until clean. Do not silence anything.
11. `uv run decree sprint status` and `uv run decree progress` — confirm the new SPEC appears in the active sprint.

Rules: never edit the old ADR/SPEC bodies; keep YAML frontmatter formatting consistent with existing docs; checkboxes in SPEC acceptance criteria must be primary (not under a Deferred section) except the explicit Deferred ones.

Return (this is machine-read): the three new document IDs with their file paths, the exact status of each (draft/accepted/...), confirmation that old ADR is superseded with bidirectional links, the final `decree lint` exit status, and a list of every file you created/modified.

4. `uv run decree new spec "Sprint Ledger v2 Storage and Item-Level Completion" --bucket sprints` — this will auto-enroll the SPEC in the active sprint via the v1 ledger; that is expected and correct.
5. Fill the three documents with real content derived from the design doc (do not copy it verbatim; write proper document prose):
   - PRD: problem = the monolithic ledger serializes parallel worktree development (the 4 mechanisms in design §1); requirements = conflict-free membership writes across worktrees and concurrent sessions, item-level mid-sprint completion, orchestrator supervision (per-worktree inspection + integration-merge lint), one-shot v1→v2 migration; success criteria as `- [ ]` checkboxes (user-observable outcomes, e.g. "two worktrees each create a SPEC and merge without touching a shared file"). Frontmatter references: [PRD-01KW1QEZEC1R1BSTZN1SSPPCEJ] (hand-edit frontmatter; there is no CLI flag).
   - ADR: Context and Problem Statement; Decision Drivers; Considered Options MUST compare: (a) keep monolith + git merge driver/union, (b) membership in document frontmatter with derived ledger, (c) multiple active sprints/lanes per worktree, (d) directory-decomposed store (state.yaml + live/<DOC-ID>.yaml + closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml) — chosen; Decision Outcome with consequences including: outcome-bearing items are not live membership; live active items are sprint-agnostic until fold (belong to whichever sprint is active at integration); completed/dropped become mid-sprint item-level outcomes; carried_over/deferred/superseded stay rollover-only; rollover remains a deliberately single-writer integration ceremony. references: [the new PRD id, PRD-01KW1QEZEC1R1BSTZN1SSPPCEJ].
   - SPEC: technical spec of design §2-§7: storage layout with YAML examples, in-memory model + per-file write API, operation semantics (init/add/backlog/draft-pool/complete/drop/pause/resume/rollover), validation invariant list (kept vs changed), CLI additions (`sprint complete`, `sprint drop`, `migrate sprint-ledger` with --dry-run|--apply and --project), migration algorithm. Acceptance criteria as `- [ ]` checkboxes covering: v2 store read/write, v1 detection error pointing at migrate command, sprint complete 100% gate + own-file-only write, sprint drop, pause folds resolved items, rollover covers only open items + creates carryover live files + archive, migration of this repo's own ledger, parallel union simulation test green, all docs+help updated. Add a small "Deferred" section for out-of-scope items (design §11). Frontmatter references: [new PRD id, new ADR id]. Frontmatter governs: [src/decree/sprints.py, src/decree/cli.py, src/decree/commands/sprint.py, src/decree/commands/new.py, src/decree/commands/migrate.py, src/decree/commands/generate_html.py, tests/test_sprint.py] (all these paths exist; governs paths must exist on disk or lint fails).
6. `uv run decree status <NEW-ADR-ID> accept`
7. `uv run decree status ADR-01KW1QGHBT2BSZ2HVT55G1QSJB accept` (it is 'proposed'; superseded is only reachable from accepted)
8. `uv run decree status ADR-01KW1QGHBT2BSZ2HVT55G1QSJB supersede <NEW-ADR-ID>` (positional target; writes bidirectional links)
9. Edit decree/spec/REDACTED.md FRONTMATTER ONLY: in its references list replace ADR-01KW1QGHBT2BSZ2HVT55G1QSJB with <NEW-ADR-ID> (references to superseded docs are hard lint errors, doubly so for active sprint items). Do not touch its body.
10. `uv run decree index regenerate`, then `uv run decree lint` — MUST exit 0. If lint reports errors, fix them (missing required sections, reference issues) and re-run until clean. Do not silence anything.
11. `uv run decree sprint status` and `uv run decree progress` — confirm the new SPEC appears in the active sprint.

Rules: never edit the old ADR/SPEC bodies; keep YAML frontmatter formatting consistent with existing docs; checkboxes in SPEC acceptance criteria must be primary (not under a Deferred section) except the explicit Deferred ones.

Return (this is machine-read): the three new document IDs with their file paths, the exact status of each (draft/accepted/...), confirmation that old ADR is superseded with bidirectional links, the final `decree lint` exit status, and a list of every file you created/modified.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end proof: 3 worker worktrees + orchestrator, zero merge conflicts,
# mid-sprint completion, integration lint, rollover. Run AFTER v2 lands.
set -euo pipefail

REPO="/Users/doruk/Desktop/SIDE_HUSTLE/decree"
SIM="$(mktemp -d /tmp/decree-sim.XXXXXX)"
D() { local dir="$1"; shift; (cd "$dir" && uv run --project "$REPO" decree "$@"); }
step() { printf '\n=== %s ===\n' "$*"; }

step "0. orchestrator repo on main"
cd "$SIM" && git init -q -b main sim && cd sim
MAIN="$SIM/sim"
D "$MAIN" init >/dev/null 2>&1 || true   # scaffold; tolerate example noise
test -f decree.toml || { echo "FATAL: decree init produced no decree.toml"; exit 1; }
D "$MAIN" sprint init "Sprint 1"
git add -A && git commit -qm "init decree + sprint"

step "1. three worker worktrees"
git worktree add -q "$SIM/wtA" -b feat-a
git worktree add -q "$SIM/wtB" -b feat-b
git worktree add -q "$SIM/wtC" -b feat-c

step "2. each worker creates its own SPEC (writes doc + one live file, nothing shared)"
for W in A B C; do
  D "$SIM/wt$W" new spec "Feature $W" --bucket "sim-$W"
done

step "3. workers mark work + commit; A completes mid-sprint"
for W in A B C; do
  WT="$SIM/wt$W"
  SPECFILE=$(ls "$WT"/decree/spec/sim-$(echo $W | tr 'A-Z' 'a-z')/*.md)
  # check every primary checkbox so 'complete' passes its 100% gate
  perl -pi -e 's/- \[ \]/- [x]/g' "$SPECFILE"
  git -C "$WT" add -A && git -C "$WT" commit -qm "spec $W"
done
SPEC_A_ID=$(grep -o 'SPEC-[0-9A-Z]*' "$SIM/wtA"/decree/spec/sim-a/*.md | head -1)
D "$SIM/wtA" sprint complete "$SPEC_A_ID"
git -C "$SIM/wtA" add -A && git -C "$SIM/wtA" commit -qm "complete $SPEC_A_ID"

step "4. same-worktree concurrency: two parallel 'new spec' in wtB"
( D "$SIM/wtB" new spec "Concurrent One" --bucket sim-b ) &
( D "$SIM/wtB" new spec "Concurrent Two" --bucket sim-b ) &
wait
LIVE_B=$(ls "$SIM/wtB"/decree/sprints/live/ | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
echo "live files in wtB: $LIVE_B (expect 3)"
git -C "$SIM/wtB" add -A && git -C "$SIM/wtB" commit -qm "concurrent specs"

step "5. orchestrator merges all three branches — MUST be conflict-free"
cd "$MAIN"
for BR in feat-a feat-b feat-c; do
  git merge -q --no-edit "$BR" || { echo "FATAL: merge conflict on $BR"; git status; exit 1; }
done

step "6. orchestrator supervision: lint + progress + status on integrated tree"
D "$MAIN" lint
D "$MAIN" sprint status
D "$MAIN" progress || true   # progress may exit nonzero only on real errors
D "$MAIN" progress --sprint "$(grep -o 'SPRINT-[0-9A-Z]*' decree/sprints/state.yaml | head -1)"

step "7. per-worktree inspection also works (orchestrator cd's into a worktree)"
D "$SIM/wtB" progress --changed --base main || D "$SIM/wtB" progress

step "8. rollover: outcomes only for OPEN items (A's is already resolved)"
OPEN_IDS=$(cd "$MAIN" && grep -L "outcome:" decree/sprints/live/*.yaml | xargs -n1 basename | sed 's/\.yaml//')
{
  echo "outcomes:"
  for ID in $OPEN_IDS; do
    printf '  %s:\n    kind: carried_over\n    reason: integration continues next sprint\n' "$ID"
  done
} > /tmp/outcomes-sim.yaml
D "$MAIN" sprint rollover "Sprint 2" --outcomes /tmp/outcomes-sim.yaml
test -n "$(ls "$MAIN"/decree/sprints/closed/)" || { echo "FATAL: no archive written"; exit 1; }
D "$MAIN" lint

step "ALL GREEN — parallel scenario proven. Sim dir: $SIM"
