You are implementing the sprint-ledger v2 redesign in /Users/doruk/Desktop/SIDE_HUSTLE/decree (Python 3.11+, uv, ruff, pytest).

THE CONTRACT: read /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-doruk-Desktop-SIDE-HUSTLE-decree/9aba7607-c379-42b0-82e5-f238fc1a47cb/scratchpad/sprint-v2-design.md FULLY and implement §2–§7 exactly. Where the design and your judgment conflict, follow the design and note the tension in your final report — do not improvise deviations. The dogfood SPEC mirroring this design is decree/spec/sprints/REDACTED.md (read it too; do NOT edit it or anything under decree/).

Read before writing: src/decree/sprints.py (entire — you are rewriting its storage layer), src/decree/commands/sprint.py, src/decree/commands/new.py, src/decree/commands/generate_html.py, src/decree/commands/migrate.py (migrate_ids_run pattern near line 694), src/decree/cli.py lines 100-320 and 900-1200, src/decree/identity.py, src/decree/log.py, src/decree/CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md.

Scope of files you may modify (nothing else):
- src/decree/sprints.py — v2 storage (state.yaml + live/<DOC-ID>.yaml + closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml), SprintState/LiveItem/LedgerView model, per-file write API with O_EXCL creates + tempfile-rename rewrites, fcntl lock contextmanager (no-op fallback without fcntl), complete_item/drop_item, reworked init/add/backlog/draft-pool/pause/resume/rollover, validate_ledger + select_sprint_scope with UNCHANGED signatures and SprintScope shape, sprint_mode_enabled covering legacy detection, v1-detected SprintLedgerError ("sprint ledger v1 detected; run `decree migrate sprint-ledger`").
- src/decree/commands/sprint.py — port all actions; add complete/drop branches; sprint status output per design §6.
- src/decree/commands/new.py — gating via cheap load_state; enrollment via create-one-live-file API; keep error texts.
- src/decree/commands/generate_html.py — build the same decree.board.v1 payload from LedgerView (synthesized active SprintRecord + ULID-sorted closed archives; active is the default selection).
- src/decree/commands/migrate.py — migrate_sprint_ledger_run per design §7 (--dry-run | --apply, --project; private v1 reader lives here or as a clearly-private section; exit 0/1/2 per the governs_run convention).
- src/decree/cli.py — sprint parser description rewrite; new `sprint complete` (positional document, repeatable --commit) and `sprint drop` (positional document, required --reason) subparsers; pause/rollover help text updates per design §6; new `migrate sprint-ledger` parser + _migrate_dispatch branch; update the migrate namespace description.

Hard requirements:
- Error-message substrings that tests and users depend on must survive verbatim where design §5/§4 says so: "completed outcome requires snapshot primary progress at 100%", "cannot be completed unless primary acceptance criteria are 100%", "must be in active sprint, backlog, or draft_pool", the paused-scope error, "sprint mode is not enabled; run `decree sprint init \"Sprint 1\"`".
- Every write path touches exactly ONE file (state transitions may write state.yaml + archive + remove folded live files — that composite runs only under the flock and only in init/pause/resume/rollover).
- Frozen dataclasses, pure validation functions taking parsed objects, log.py info/error/success to stderr, machine output to stdout, exit codes 0/1 (2 for migrate config errors). Match existing code style; comments only for non-obvious constraints.
- Keep `load_outcomes_file`, `document_context_for`, `_snapshot_for_doc`, checkbox-snapshot logic working as today.

Verification loop (run until ALL green; do not stop early, do not skip):
1. `uv run ruff check src/ --fix` and `uv run ruff format src/` — clean.

<result>Implementation complete and all gates green. Final report:

## Per-file changes

**`src/decree/sprints.py`** — full rewrite of the storage layer to the v2 directory store (`decree/sprints/state.yaml` + `live/<DOC-ID>.yaml` + `closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml`, schema `decree.sprints.v2`). New frozen dataclasses `SprintState`, `LiveItem`, `LedgerView`; `SprintRecord`/`SprintItem` kept for archives. Per-file write API (O_EXCL creates, tempfile+rename rewrites), `_ledger_lock` fcntl contextmanager (no-op without fcntl, used only by init/pause/resume/rollover), new `complete_item`/`drop_item`, reworked init/add/backlog/draft-pool/pause/resume/rollover, validation rebased per §5, v1 detection everywhere. `load_outcomes_file`, `document_context_for`, `_snapshot_for_doc`, checkbox-snapshot logic unchanged.

**`src/decree/commands/new.py`** — gating via cheap `load_state()`; enrollment still goes through `add_to_active_sprint`/`add_to_backlog`/`add_to_draft_pool` (each now writes exactly one live file). All error texts verbatim.

**`src/decree/commands/generate_html.py`** — board built from `load_view`: closed archives ULID-sorted, synthesized active `SprintRecord` (from `state.active` + live active items as `SprintItem`s incl. outcomes) appended last; backlog/draft_pool from live files. `decree.board.v1` payload unchanged; active is default selection with last-closed fallback.

**`src/decree/commands/migrate.py`** — new section with `migrate_sprint_ledger_run` + private v1 reader (`_plan_sprint_ledger_migration`, `_apply_sprint_ledger_migration`, `SprintLedgerPlan`, `_load_corpus`, `_iso_date`). `--dry-run` prints plan to stdout, writes nothing (exit 0); `--apply` guards (state.yaml present → 2; ledger.yaml missing → 2; malformed v1 → 2), writes via the sprints.py public write API, deletes ledger.yaml, runs `validate_ledger` (errors → 1, clean → 0).

**`src/decree/cli.py`** — sprint parser description rewritten for the directory store; new `sprint complete DOCUMENT [--commit SHA]…` and `sprint drop DOCUMENT --reason TEXT` subparsers; pause/rollover help updated per §6; new `migrate sprint-ledger` parser (required mutually-exclusive `--dry-run|--apply`, `--project`, no `--json`) + dispatch branch; migrate namespace description now enumerates all four sub-actions.

## Public API of sprints.py v2

```python
# constants
SPRINTS_REL_PATH, STATE_REL_PATH, LIVE_REL_PATH, CLOSED_REL_PATH, LEDGER_REL_PATH  # v1, detection/migration only
SCHEMA = "decree.sprints.v2"; MODE_ENABLED; BACKLOG_WARN_AFTER_DAYS = 30
ITEM_KINDS; LIVE_SCOPES; OUTCOME_KINDS; LIVE_OUTCOME_KINDS = {"completed", "dropped"}; OUTCOMES_REQUIRING_REASON
V1_DETECTED_MESSAGE; NOT_ENABLED_MESSAGE

# model
class SprintLedgerError(ValueError)
@dataclass(frozen=True) SprintState(schema, mode, state, active: dict|None, paused: dict|None)  # .from_raw(raw), .to_dict()
@dataclass(frozen=True) LiveItem(document, scope, kind="execution", source="manual", added=today, since=None, reason=None, review_after=None, carryover_from=None, outcome=None)  # .from_raw(raw, *, where), .to_dict()
@dataclass(frozen=True) SprintItem(...)   # unchanged v1 shape
@dataclass(frozen=True) SprintRecord(...) # unchanged v1 shape
@dataclass(frozen=True) LedgerView(state, live: dict[str, LiveItem], closed: tuple[SprintRecord, ...])
    # properties: active_items, active_open_items, active_done_items, backlog_items, draft_pool_items
    # live_membership() -> dict[str, str]  (doc -> scope, outcome-less only)
@dataclass(frozen=True) SprintValidation(errors, warnings)
@dataclass(frozen=True) SprintScope(...)  # UNCHANGED shape

# paths / detection
def sprints_path(root=None) -> Path; state_path(root=None); live_path(root=None); closed_path(root=None); ledger_path(root=None)
def sprint_mode_enabled(root=None) -> bool   # True if state.yaml OR legacy ledger.yaml

# I/O (each write touches exactly one file)
def load_state(root=None) -> SprintState
def load_view(root=None) -> LedgerView
def save_state(state: SprintState, root=None) -> None
def create_live_item(item: LiveItem, root=None) -> None      # O_CREAT|O_EXCL
def rewrite_live_item(item: LiveItem, root=None) -> None     # tempfile+rename
def remove_live_item(doc_id: str, root=None) -> None         # fold-time only
def write_closed_sprint(record: SprintRecord, root=None) -> None  # O_EXCL, append-only

# operations
def init_ledger(name, *, root=None, today=None) -> SprintState
def add_to_active_sprint(document, *, kind="execution", source="manual", root=None, today=None) -> LiveItem
def add_to_backlog(document, *, reason, kind="execution", source="manual", root=None, today=None, review_after=None) -> LiveItem
def add_to_draft_pool(document, *, reason, kind="execution", root=None, today=None) -> LiveItem
def complete_item(document, *, commits: tuple[str, ...] = (), root=None, today=None) -> LiveItem
def drop_item(document, *, reason, root=None, today=None) -> LiveItem
def pause_ledger(reason, *, root=None, today=None) -> SprintState
def resume_ledger(name, *, root=None, today=None) -> SprintState
def rollover_ledger(name, outcomes, docs, *, root=None, today=None) -> SprintState
def load_outcomes_file(path) -> dict[str, dict]              # unchanged

# validation / scoping (UNCHANGED signatures)
def validate_ledger(root: Path, docs: list) -> SprintValidation
def select_sprint_scope(docs, args, *, root=None) -> SprintScope | None
def document_context_for(items, by_id) -> tuple
```

## Deviations from the design doc

1. **`add_to_draft_pool` keeps the v1 signature** (no `source` kwarg) — §4 wrote `add_to_draft_pool(...)` ambiguously; v1 parity minimizes churn. `LiveItem.to_dict()` omits empty `source` so migrated v1 draft-pool items (which had no source field) don't gain `source: ''`.
2. **Closed-archive filename↔id check added** (`closed/<name>.yaml: filename stem must equal sprint id …`) — not explicitly in §5, but carryover linearity depends on filename order, so a mismatch must surface.
3. **Migration guards apply to `--dry-run` too** — §7 specified the guards for `--apply`; a dry-run with no v1 ledger (or v2 present) also exits 2 since there's nothing to plan. This is what makes "second `--apply` exits 2" work (ledger.yaml is gone).
4. **`pause` writes the archive even with zero items** — preserves sprint history (v1 kept the closed record in the monolith).
5. **New `_stringify_dates` normalization** on outcome dicts at parse time — YAML unquoted dates became `datetime.date` objects and broke the JSON board (latent v1 bug, surfaced by the smoke test).
6. **`init_ledger` ordering**: existing state.yaml → `"sprint ledger already exists"` (v1 message); only-v1-ledger → the v1-detected message.
7. **`sprint status`** keeps v1's `Tasks:`/`Planning:` grouping for open items (design only said "open items"), adding the `Done (awaiting rollover):` section.

## Smoke results (throwaway tmp projects, via the installed CLI)

**68/68 assertions passed**, including: init creates state.yaml+live/+closed/ and lints clean; `new spec` auto-enrolls exactly one live file with `scope: active, source: new, kind: execution`; planning PRD add; backlog/draft-pool with reasons; complete <100% refused with the exact gate message; complete at 100% writes `outcome{kind: completed, evidence.commits: [abc123], snapshot 1/1}` into **only** its own live file (all other live files byte-identical, state.yaml untouched, closed/ empty); drop records reason+snapshot; re-complete refused ("already has a recorded outcome"); pause refused with open items (exact new message); rollover with outcomes for open items only — archive folds pre-resolved outcomes verbatim, `carried_over.to_sprint` = new id, carryover live file has `carryover_from`, state.active updated; pause folds resolved carryover into second archive; paused `new spec` refused / `--backlog` works; resume; **lint exit 0 at every green step**; v1 detection: `sprint status`, `new spec`, and `lint` all exit 1 with `sprint ledger v1 detected; run \`decree migrate sprint-ledger\``; migrate `--dry-run` writes nothing; `--apply` on a dogfood-shaped v1 ledger (closed sprint w/ completed+carried_over incl. snapshots, active carryover, backlog, draft_pool) produces a store that lints clean with all fields preserved; second `--apply` exits 2; no-ledger guard exits 2; generate-html board is `decree.board.v1` with archives ULID-sorted, active last and selected. Also verified `progress` (human+JSON, scope `active sprint SPRINT-… (Sprint 1)`) and `ddd` work against the v2 store.

## Gate confirmation

- `uv run ruff check src/ --fix` + `uv run ruff format src/` — clean, no changes.
- `uv run pytest -q --ignore=tests/test_sprint.py --ignore=tests/test_generate_html.py` — **729 passed**.
- `uv run decree lint` in this repo fails with the v1 migrate hint — intended; left untouched. (The dirty `decree/` files in git status predate this task — Phase 2 dogfooding.)

## For the tests/docs phases

- **New CLI help strings** (exact): sprint parser description "Manage the sprint directory store at decree/sprints/: state.yaml holds the lifecycle state, live/<DOC-ID>.yaml holds one file per live membership, and closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml holds one archive per closed sprint. Sprint mode is disabled until \`decree sprint init\` creates state.yaml."; `complete` help "Record a completed outcome for one active sprint item mid-sprint (requires 100% primary acceptance criteria)" (`%%`-escaped in source); `drop` help "Record a dropped outcome for one active sprint item mid-sprint"; pause help "Pause sprint mode after every active item is completed, dropped, or rolled over"; rollover `--outcomes` help now begins "YAML file mapping each OPEN active sprint document (items completed or dropped mid-sprint are already resolved) to …".
- **New error messages introduced**: `"{DOC} is not an active sprint item"`, `"{DOC} is in {scope}, not the active sprint"`, `"{DOC} already has a recorded outcome ({kind})"`, `"cannot pause with open active-sprint items; complete, drop, or rollover them first"`, `"{DOC} already has a resolved live record ({kind}); resolved record folds at next rollover"`, `"{DOC} is already in {active sprint|backlog|draft_pool}"`, `"live/<f>.yaml: filename stem must equal document field {DOC}"`, `"live/<f>.yaml: scope must be one of […]"`, `"live/<f>.yaml: outcome is only allowed on scope=active items"`, `"live/<f>.yaml: live outcome kind must be one of ['completed', 'dropped']"`, `"live/<f>.yaml: scope=active live item is not allowed while sprint mode is paused"`, `"closed/<f>.yaml: closed archive must have status closed"`, `"closed/<f>.yaml: archive id matches the active sprint id {ID}"`, `"closed/<f>.yaml: filename stem must equal sprint id {ID}"`, `"closed sprint archive already exists: {path}"`, migrate guards `"sprint ledger v2 already present at decree/sprints/state.yaml; nothing to migrate"` / `"no v1 sprint ledger found at decree/sprints/ledger.yaml; nothing to migrate"`.
- **Live-file validation `where` prefixes are file-based** (`decree/sprints/live/<DOC-ID>.yaml: …`), so v1's `"backlog {DOC}: …"` prefixes are gone — but load-bearing substrings ("backlog item is … days old", "terminal SPEC requires explicit reopen semantics", "completed outcome requires snapshot primary progress at 100%", "must be in active sprint, backlog, or draft_pool", stale-reference wording) survive verbatim.
- **`sprint status` stdout**: `Sprint mode: {state}` / `Active: {id} {name} ({n} items)` / `Tasks:`+`Planning:` (open only) / `Done (awaiting rollover):` with `  {DOC} ({kind})` lines / `Backlog: {n} items` / `Draft pool: {n} items`.
- Rollover/complete share the exact v1 gate message `"{DOC} cannot be completed unless primary acceptance criteria are 100%"`; rollover missing/extra messages unchanged.
- `select_sprint_scope --sprint <active-id>` includes resolved items; default active scope excludes them; `--all-sprints` = archives + all active live items.</result>

```python
    # properties: active_items, active_open_items, active_done_items, backlog_items, draft_pool_items
    # live_membership() -> dict[str, str]  (doc -> scope, outcome-less only)
@dataclass(frozen=True) SprintValidation(errors, warnings)
@dataclass(frozen=True) SprintScope(...)  # UNCHANGED shape

# paths / detection
def sprints_path(root=None) -> Path; state_path(root=None); live_path(root=None); closed_path(root=None); ledger_path(root=None)
def sprint_mode_enabled(root=None) -> bool   # True if state.yaml OR legacy ledger.yaml

# I/O (each write touches exactly one file)
def load_state(root=None) -> SprintState
def load_view(root=None) -> LedgerView
def save_state(state: SprintState, root=None) -> None
def create_live_item(item: LiveItem, root=None) -> None      # O_CREAT|O_EXCL
def rewrite_live_item(item: LiveItem, root=None) -> None     # tempfile+rename
def remove_live_item(doc_id: str, root=None) -> None         # fold-time only
def write_closed_sprint(record: SprintRecord, root=None) -> None  # O_EXCL, append-only

# operations
def init_ledger(name, *, root=None, today=None) -> SprintState
def add_to_active_sprint(document, *, kind="execution", source="manual", root=None, today=None) -> LiveItem
def add_to_backlog(document, *, reason, kind="execution", source="manual", root=None, today=None, review_after=None) -> LiveItem
def add_to_draft_pool(document, *, reason, kind="execution", root=None, today=None) -> LiveItem
def complete_item(document, *, commits: tuple[str, ...] = (), root=None, today=None) -> LiveItem
def drop_item(document, *, reason, root=None, today=None) -> LiveItem
def pause_ledger(reason, *, root=None, today=None) -> SprintState
def resume_ledger(name, *, root=None, today=None) -> SprintState
def rollover_ledger(name, outcomes, docs, *, root=None, today=None) -> SprintState
def load_outcomes_file(path) -> dict[str, dict]              # unchanged

# validation / scoping (UNCHANGED signatures)
def validate_ledger(root: Path, docs: list) -> SprintValidation
def select_sprint_scope(docs, args, *, root=None) -> SprintScope | None
def document_context_for(items, by_id) -> tuple
```

## Deviations from the design doc

1. **`add_to_draft_pool` keeps the v1 signature** (no `source` kwarg) — §4 wrote `add_to_draft_pool(...)` ambiguously; v1 parity minimizes churn. `LiveItem.to_dict()` omits empty `source` so migrated v1 draft-pool items (which had no source field) don't gain `source: ''`.
2. **Closed-archive filename↔id check added** (`closed/<name>.yaml: filename stem must equal sprint id …`) — not explicitly in §5, but carryover linearity depends on filename order, so a mismatch must surface.
3. **Migration guards apply to `--dry-run` too** — §7 specified the guards for `--apply`; a dry-run with no v1 ledger (or v2 present) also exits 2 since there's nothing to plan. This is what makes "second `--apply` exits 2" work (ledger.yaml is gone).
4. **`pause` writes the archive even with zero items** — preserves sprint history (v1 kept the closed record in the monolith).
5. **New `_stringify_dates` normalization** on outcome dicts at parse time — YAML unquoted dates became `datetime.date` objects and broke the JSON board (latent v1 bug, surfaced by the smoke test).
6. **`init_ledger` ordering**: existing state.yaml → `"sprint ledger already exists"` (v1 message); only-v1-ledger → the v1-detected message.
7. **`sprint status`** keeps v1's `Tasks:`/`Planning:` grouping for open items (design only said "open items"), adding the `Done (awaiting rollover):` section.

## Smoke results (throwaway tmp projects, via the installed CLI)

**68/68 assertions passed**, including: init creates state.yaml+live/+closed/ and lints clean; `new spec` auto-enrolls exactly one live file with `scope: active, source: new, kind: execution`; planning PRD add; backlog/draft-pool with reasons; complete <100% refused with the exact gate message; complete at 100% writes `outcome{kind: completed, evidence.commits: [abc123], snapshot 1/1}` into **only** its own live file (all other live files byte-identical, state.yaml untouched, closed/ empty); drop records reason+snapshot; re-complete refused ("already has a recorded outcome"); pause refused with open items (exact new message); rollover with outcomes for open items only — archive folds pre-resolved outcomes verbatim, `carried_over.to_sprint` = new id, carryover live file has `carryover_from`, state.active updated; pause folds resolved carryover into second archive; paused `new spec` refused / `--backlog` works; resume; **lint exit 0 at every green step**; v1 detection: `sprint status`, `new spec`, and `lint` all exit 1 with `sprint ledger v1 detected; run \`decree migrate sprint-ledger\``; migrate `--dry-run` writes nothing; `--apply` on a dogfood-shaped v1 ledger (closed sprint w/ completed+carried_over incl. snapshots, active carryover, backlog, draft_pool) produces a store that lints clean with all fields preserved; second `--apply` exits 2; no-ledger guard exits 2; generate-html board is `decree.board.v1` with archives ULID-sorted, active last and selected. Also verified `progress` (human+JSON, scope `active sprint SPRINT-… (Sprint 1)`) and `ddd` work against the v2 store.

## Gate confirmation

- `uv run ruff check src/ --fix` + `uv run ruff format src/` — clean, no changes.
- `uv run pytest -q --ignore=tests/test_sprint.py --ignore=tests/test_generate_html.py` — **729 passed**.
- `uv run decree lint` in this repo fails with the v1 migrate hint — intended; left untouched. (The dirty `decree/` files in git status predate this task — Phase 2 dogfooding.)

## For the tests/docs phases

- **New CLI help strings** (exact): sprint parser description "Manage the sprint directory store at decree/sprints/: state.yaml holds the lifecycle state, live/<DOC-ID>.yaml holds one file per live membership, and closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml holds one archive per closed sprint. Sprint mode is disabled until \`decree sprint init\` creates state.yaml."; `complete` help "Record a completed outcome for one active sprint item mid-sprint (requires 100% primary acceptance criteria)" (`%%`-escaped in source); `drop` help "Record a dropped outcome for one active sprint item mid-sprint"; pause help "Pause sprint mode after every active item is completed, dropped, or rolled over"; rollover `--outcomes` help now begins "YAML file mapping each OPEN active sprint document (items completed or dropped mid-sprint are already resolved) to …".
- **New error messages introduced**: `"{DOC} is not an active sprint item"`, `"{DOC} is in {scope}, not the active sprint"`, `"{DOC} already has a recorded outcome ({kind})"`, `"cannot pause with open active-sprint items; complete, drop, or rollover them first"`, `"{DOC} already has a resolved live record ({kind}); resolved record folds at next rollover"`, `"{DOC} is already in {active sprint|backlog|draft_pool}"`, `"live/<f>.yaml: filename stem must equal document field {DOC}"`, `"live/<f>.yaml: scope must be one of […]"`, `"live/<f>.yaml: outcome is only allowed on scope=active items"`, `"live/<f>.yaml: live outcome kind must be one of ['completed', 'dropped']"`, `"live/<f>.yaml: scope=active live item is not allowed while sprint mode is paused"`, `"closed/<f>.yaml: closed archive must have status closed"`, `"closed/<f>.yaml: archive id matches the active sprint id {ID}"`, `"closed/<f>.yaml: filename stem must equal sprint id {ID}"`, `"closed sprint archive already exists: {path}"`, migrate guards `"sprint ledger v2 already present at decree/sprints/state.yaml; nothing to migrate"` / `"no v1 sprint ledger found at decree/sprints/ledger.yaml; nothing to migrate"`.
- **Live-file validation `where` prefixes are file-based** (`decree/sprints/live/<DOC-ID>.yaml: …`), so v1's `"backlog {DOC}: …"` prefixes are gone — but load-bearing substrings ("backlog item is … days old", "terminal SPEC requires explicit reopen semantics", "completed outcome requires snapshot primary progress at 100%", "must be in active sprint, backlog, or draft_pool", stale-reference wording) survive verbatim.
- **`sprint status` stdout**: `Sprint mode: {state}` / `Active: {id} {name} ({n} items)` / `Tasks:`+`Planning:` (open only) / `Done (awaiting rollover):` with `  {DOC} ({kind})` lines / `Backlog: {n} items` / `Draft pool: {n} items`.
- Rollover/complete share the exact v1 gate message `"{DOC} cannot be completed unless primary acceptance criteria are 100%"`; rollover missing/extra messages unchanged.
- `select_sprint_scope --sprint <active-id>` includes resolved items; default active scope excludes them; `--all-sprints` = archives + all active live items.</result>

input.content:
# Sprint Ledger v2 — Implementation Facts (from the landed core change)

Companion to sprint-v2-design.md (the contract). This file records what the
implementation ACTUALLY exposes, for test/docs agents. The repo's own ledger is
ALREADY migrated to v2 (decree/sprints/state.yaml + live/ + closed/).

## Public API of src/decree/sprints.py (v2)

Constants: SPRINTS_REL_PATH, STATE_REL_PATH, LIVE_REL_PATH, CLOSED_REL_PATH,
LEDGER_REL_PATH (v1, detection/migration only), SCHEMA="decree.sprints.v2",
MODE_ENABLED, BACKLOG_WARN_AFTER_DAYS=30, ITEM_KINDS, LIVE_SCOPES,
OUTCOME_KINDS, LIVE_OUTCOME_KINDS={"completed","dropped"},
OUTCOMES_REQUIRING_REASON, V1_DETECTED_MESSAGE, NOT_ENABLED_MESSAGE.

Model: SprintLedgerError(ValueError); frozen dataclasses SprintState(schema,
mode, state, active: dict|None, paused: dict|None), LiveItem(document, scope,
kind="execution", source="manual", added=today, since=None, reason=None,
review_after=None, carryover_from=None, outcome=None), SprintItem/SprintRecord
(unchanged v1 shapes, archives only), LedgerView(state, live: dict[str,
LiveItem], closed: tuple[SprintRecord,...]) with properties active_items,
active_open_items, active_done_items, backlog_items, draft_pool_items and
live_membership() -> dict[doc,scope] (outcome-less only), SprintValidation,
SprintScope (UNCHANGED shape).

Paths/detection: sprints_path/state_path/live_path/closed_path/ledger_path
(root=None); sprint_mode_enabled(root) True if state.yaml OR legacy ledger.yaml.

I/O (each write = one file): load_state, load_view, save_state,
create_live_item (O_CREAT|O_EXCL), rewrite_live_item (tempfile+rename),
remove_live_item (fold only), write_closed_sprint (O_EXCL append-only).

Operations: init_ledger(name) -> SprintState; add_to_active_sprint(document,
kind, source, root, today) -> LiveItem; add_to_backlog(document, reason, kind,
source, root, today, review_after) -> LiveItem; add_to_draft_pool(document,
reason, kind, root, today) -> LiveItem (NO source kwarg, v1 parity);
complete_item(document, commits=(), root, today) -> LiveItem;
drop_item(document, reason, root, today) -> LiveItem; pause_ledger(reason);
resume_ledger(name); rollover_ledger(name, outcomes, docs);
load_outcomes_file(path) unchanged.

Unchanged signatures: validate_ledger(root, docs) -> SprintValidation;
select_sprint_scope(docs, args, *, root=None) -> SprintScope|None;
document_context_for(items, by_id).

Scope semantics: default active scope = open (outcome-less) scope=active items;
--sprint <active-id> INCLUDES resolved items; --all-sprints = archives + all
active live items; --backlog/--draft-pool by scope; paused default errors as v1.

## Exact CLI help strings (already in cli.py)

- sprint parser description: "Manage the sprint directory store at
  decree/sprints/: state.yaml holds the lifecycle state, live/<DOC-ID>.yaml
  holds one file per live membership, and closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml holds one
  archive per closed sprint. Sprint mode is disabled until `decree sprint init`
  creates state.yaml."
- `sprint complete DOCUMENT [--commit SHA]...` help: "Record a completed
  outcome for one active sprint item mid-sprint (requires 100% primary
  acceptance criteria)"
- `sprint drop DOCUMENT --reason TEXT` help: "Record a dropped outcome for one
  active sprint item mid-sprint"
- pause help: "Pause sprint mode after every active item is completed, dropped,
  or rolled over"
- rollover --outcomes help starts: "YAML file mapping each OPEN active sprint
  document (items completed or dropped mid-sprint are already resolved) to ..."
- `migrate sprint-ledger`: required mutually-exclusive --dry-run | --apply,
  plus --project (default cwd); NO --json. Exit: 0 ok, 1 post-apply validation
  errors, 2 guard/config errors.

## Exact messages (load-bearing for tests and docs)

Survived from v1 verbatim: "completed outcome requires snapshot primary
progress at 100%", "{DOC} cannot be completed unless primary acceptance
criteria are 100%" (shared by rollover AND complete), "must be in active
sprint, backlog, or draft_pool", "backlog item is ... days old", "terminal SPEC
requires explicit reopen semantics", stale-reference wording, "sprint mode is
not enabled; run `decree sprint init \"Sprint 1\"`", rollover missing/extra
outcome messages, "sprint ledger already exists" (existing state.yaml).

New: "sprint ledger v1 detected; run `decree migrate sprint-ledger`";
"{DOC} is not an active sprint item"; "{DOC} is in {scope}, not the active
sprint"; "{DOC} already has a recorded outcome ({kind})"; "cannot pause with
open active-sprint items; complete, drop, or rollover them first"; "{DOC}
already has a resolved live record ({kind}); resolved record folds at next
rollover"; "{DOC} is already in {active sprint|backlog|draft_pool}";
"live/<f>.yaml: filename stem must equal document field {DOC}"; "live/<f>.yaml:
scope must be one of [...]"; "live/<f>.yaml: outcome is only allowed on
scope=active items"; "live/<f>.yaml: live outcome kind must be one of
['completed', 'dropped']"; "live/<f>.yaml: scope=active live item is not
allowed while sprint mode is paused"; "closed/<f>.yaml: closed archive must
have status closed"; "closed/<f>.yaml: archive id matches the active sprint id
{ID}"; "closed/<f>.yaml: filename stem must equal sprint id {ID}"; "closed
sprint archive already exists: {path}"; migrate guards: "sprint ledger v2
already present at decree/sprints/state.yaml; nothing to migrate" / "no v1
sprint ledger found at decree/sprints/ledger.yaml; nothing to migrate".
NOTE: live-file validation prefixes are file-based
("decree/sprints/live/<DOC-ID>.yaml: ...") — v1's "backlog {DOC}:" prefixes are gone.

## sprint status stdout shape

"Sprint mode: {state}" / "Active: {id} {name} ({n} items)" / "Tasks:" +
"Planning:" (open only) / "Done (awaiting rollover):" with "  {DOC} ({kind})"
lines / "Backlog: {n} items" / "Draft pool: {n} items".

## Behavior notes

- pause writes the archive even with zero items (history preserved).
- Migration guards apply to --dry-run too (no v1 file or v2 present -> exit 2).
- Outcome date fields are normalized to ISO strings at parse time
  (_stringify_dates) — fixes a latent v1 bug where YAML dates broke the JSON board.
- generate-html: decree.board.v1 payload unchanged; closed archives ULID-sorted,
  synthesized active record appended last and selected by default.
- mcp_server.py untouched (insulated behind progress_for_scope).

## Dogfood corpus (current state)

- PRD-01KWKXH2EX1WH7XWXCNKSMRW24 (draft) decree/prd/sprints/...parallel-safe-sprint-execution.md
- ADR-01KWKXH8423XTBV6CDVXB4B2ZB (accepted) decree/adr/sprints/...directory-decomposed-sprint-ledger.md
  — supersedes ADR-01KW1QGHBT2BSZ2HVT55G1QSJB (superseded)
- SPEC-01KWKXHERB56W94SCRZEVMBQMJ (draft, active sprint item, 9 primary + 6 deferred ACs)
  decree/spec/sprints/...sprint-ledger-v2-storage-and-item-level-completion.md
- Repo ledger migrated: decree/sprints/{state.yaml, live/*5, closed/*1}; lint exit 0.

## Exact CLI help strings (already in cli.py)

- sprint parser description: "Manage the sprint directory store at
  decree/sprints/: state.yaml holds the lifecycle state, live/<DOC-ID>.yaml
  holds one file per live membership, and closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml holds one
  archive per closed sprint. Sprint mode is disabled until `decree sprint init`
  creates state.yaml."
- `sprint complete DOCUMENT [--commit SHA]...` help: "Record a completed
  outcome for one active sprint item mid-sprint (requires 100% primary
  acceptance criteria)"
- `sprint drop DOCUMENT --reason TEXT` help: "Record a dropped outcome for one
  active sprint item mid-sprint"
- pause help: "Pause sprint mode after every active item is completed, dropped,
  or rolled over"
- rollover --outcomes help starts: "YAML file mapping each OPEN active sprint
  document (items completed or dropped mid-sprint are already resolved) to ..."
- `migrate sprint-ledger`: required mutually-exclusive --dry-run | --apply,
  plus --project (default cwd); NO --json. Exit: 0 ok, 1 post-apply validation
  errors, 2 guard/config errors.

## Exact messages (load-bearing for tests and docs)

Survived from v1 verbatim: "completed outcome requires snapshot primary
progress at 100%", "{DOC} cannot be completed unless primary acceptance
criteria are 100%" (shared by rollover AND complete), "must be in active
sprint, backlog, or draft_pool", "backlog item is ... days old", "terminal SPEC
requires explicit reopen semantics", stale-reference wording, "sprint mode is
not enabled; run `decree sprint init \"Sprint 1\"`", rollover missing/extra
outcome messages, "sprint ledger already exists" (existing state.yaml).

New: "sprint ledger v1 detected; run `decree migrate sprint-ledger`";
"{DOC} is not an active sprint item"; "{DOC} is in {scope}, not the active
sprint"; "{DOC} already has a recorded outcome ({kind})"; "cannot pause with
open active-sprint items; complete, drop, or rollover them first"; "{DOC}
already has a resolved live record ({kind}); resolved record folds at next
rollover"; "{DOC} is already in {active sprint|backlog|draft_pool}";
"live/<f>.yaml: filename stem must equal document field {DOC}"; "live/<f>.yaml:
scope must be one of [...]"; "live/<f>.yaml: outcome is only allowed on
scope=active items"; "live/<f>.yaml: live outcome kind must be one of
['completed', 'dropped']"; "live/<f>.yaml: scope=active live item is not
allowed while sprint mode is paused"; "closed/<f>.yaml: closed archive must
have status closed"; "closed/<f>.yaml: archive id matches the active sprint id
{ID}"; "closed/<f>.yaml: filename stem must equal sprint id {ID}"; "closed
sprint archive already exists: {path}"; migrate guards: "sprint ledger v2
already present at decree/sprints/state.yaml; nothing to migrate" / "no v1
sprint ledger found at decree/sprints/ledger.yaml; nothing to migrate".
NOTE: live-file validation prefixes are file-based
("decree/sprints/live/<DOC-ID>.yaml: ...") — v1's "backlog {DOC}:" prefixes are gone.

## sprint status stdout shape

"Sprint mode: {state}" / "Active: {id} {name} ({n} items)" / "Tasks:" +
"Planning:" (open only) / "Done (awaiting rollover):" with "  {DOC} ({kind})"
lines / "Backlog: {n} items" / "Draft pool: {n} items".

## Behavior notes

- pause writes the archive even with zero items (history preserved).
- Migration guards apply to --dry-run too (no v1 file or v2 present -> exit 2).
- Outcome date fields are normalized to ISO strings at parse time
  (_stringify_dates) — fixes a latent v1 bug where YAML dates broke the JSON board.
- generate-html: decree.board.v1 payload unchanged; closed archives ULID-sorted,
  synthesized active record appended last and selected by default.
- mcp_server.py untouched (insulated behind progress_for_scope).

## Dogfood corpus (current state)

- PRD-01KWKXH2EX1WH7XWXCNKSMRW24 (draft) decree/prd/sprints/...parallel-safe-sprint-execution.md
- ADR-01KWKXH8423XTBV6CDVXB4B2ZB (accepted) decree/adr/sprints/...directory-decomposed-sprint-ledger.md
  — supersedes ADR-01KW1QGHBT2BSZ2HVT55G1QSJB (superseded)
- SPEC-01KWKXHERB56W94SCRZEVMBQMJ (draft, active sprint item, 9 primary + 6 deferred ACs)
  decree/spec/sprints/...sprint-ledger-v2-storage-and-item-level-completion.md
- Repo ledger migrated: decree/sprints/{state.yaml, live/*5, closed/*1}; lint exit 0.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end proof: 3 worker worktrees + orchestrator, zero merge conflicts,
# mid-sprint completion, integration lint, rollover. Run AFTER v2 lands.
set -euo pipefail

REPO="/Users/doruk/Desktop/SIDE_HUSTLE/decree"
SIM="$(mktemp -d /tmp/decree-sim.XXXXXX)"
D() { local dir="$1"; shift; (cd "$dir" && uv run --project "$REPO" decree "$@"); }
step() { printf '\n=== %s ===\n' "$*"; }

step "0. orchestrator repo on main"
cd "$SIM" && git init -q -b main sim && cd sim
MAIN="$SIM/sim"
D "$MAIN" init >/dev/null 2>&1 || true   # scaffold; tolerate example noise
test -f decree.toml || { echo "FATAL: decree init produced no decree.toml"; exit 1; }
D "$MAIN" sprint init "Sprint 1"
git add -A && git commit -qm "init decree + sprint"

step "1. three worker worktrees"
git worktree add -q "$SIM/wtA" -b feat-a
git worktree add -q "$SIM/wtB" -b feat-b
git worktree add -q "$SIM/wtC" -b feat-c

step "2. each worker creates its own SPEC (writes doc + one live file, nothing shared)"
for W in A B C; do
  D "$SIM/wt$W" new spec "Feature $W" --bucket "sim-$W"
done

step "3. workers mark work + commit; A completes mid-sprint"
for W in A B C; do
  WT="$SIM/wt$W"
  SPECFILE=$(ls "$WT"/decree/spec/sim-$(echo $W | tr 'A-Z' 'a-z')/*.md)
  # check every primary checkbox so 'complete' passes its 100% gate
  perl -pi -e 's/- \[ \]/- [x]/g' "$SPECFILE"
  git -C "$WT" add -A && git -C "$WT" commit -qm "spec $W"
done
SPEC_A_ID=$(grep -o 'SPEC-[0-9A-Z]*' "$SIM/wtA"/decree/spec/sim-a/*.md | head -1)
D "$SIM/wtA" sprint complete "$SPEC_A_ID"
git -C "$SIM/wtA" add -A && git -C "$SIM/wtA" commit -qm "complete $SPEC_A_ID"

step "4. same-worktree concurrency: two parallel 'new spec' in wtB"
( D "$SIM/wtB" new spec "Concurrent One" --bucket sim-b ) &
( D "$SIM/wtB" new spec "Concurrent Two" --bucket sim-b ) &
wait
LIVE_B=$(ls "$SIM/wtB"/decree/sprints/live/ | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
echo "live files in wtB: $LIVE_B (expect 3)"
git -C "$SIM/wtB" add -A && git -C "$SIM/wtB" commit -qm "concurrent specs"

step "5. orchestrator merges all three branches — MUST be conflict-free"
cd "$MAIN"
for BR in feat-a feat-b feat-c; do
  git merge -q --no-edit "$BR" || { echo "FATAL: merge conflict on $BR"; git status; exit 1; }
done

step "6. orchestrator supervision: lint + progress + status on integrated tree"
D "$MAIN" lint
D "$MAIN" sprint status
D "$MAIN" progress || true   # progress may exit nonzero only on real errors
D "$MAIN" progress --sprint "$(grep -o 'SPRINT-[0-9A-Z]*' decree/sprints/state.yaml | head -1)"

step "7. per-worktree inspection also works (orchestrator cd's into a worktree)"
D "$SIM/wtB" progress --changed --base main || D "$SIM/wtB" progress

step "8. rollover: outcomes only for OPEN items (A's is already resolved)"
OPEN_IDS=$(cd "$MAIN" && grep -L "outcome:" decree/sprints/live/*.yaml | xargs -n1 basename | sed 's/\.yaml//')
{
  echo "outcomes:"
  for ID in $OPEN_IDS; do
    printf '  %s:\n    kind: carried_over\n    reason: integration continues next sprint\n' "$ID"
  done
} > /tmp/outcomes-sim.yaml
D "$MAIN" sprint rollover "Sprint 2" --outcomes /tmp/outcomes-sim.yaml
test -n "$(ls "$MAIN"/decree/sprints/closed/)" || { echo "FATAL: no archive written"; exit 1; }
D "$MAIN" lint

step "ALL GREEN — parallel scenario proven. Sim dir: $SIM"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end proof: 3 worker worktrees + orchestrator, zero merge conflicts,
# mid-sprint completion, integration lint, rollover. Run AFTER v2 lands.
set -euo pipefail

REPO="/Users/doruk/Desktop/SIDE_HUSTLE/decree"
SIM="$(mktemp -d /tmp/decree-sim.XXXXXX)"
D() { local dir="$1"; shift; (cd "$dir" && uv run --project "$REPO" decree "$@"); }
step() { printf '\n=== %s ===\n' "$*"; }

step "0. orchestrator repo on main"
cd "$SIM" && git init -q -b main sim && cd sim
MAIN="$SIM/sim"
D "$MAIN" init >/dev/null 2>&1 || true   # scaffold; tolerate example noise
test -f decree.toml || { echo "FATAL: decree init produced no decree.toml"; exit 1; }
D "$MAIN" sprint init "Sprint 1"
git add -A && git commit -qm "init decree + sprint"

step "1. three worker worktrees"
git worktree add -q "$SIM/wtA" -b feat-a
git worktree add -q "$SIM/wtB" -b feat-b
git worktree add -q "$SIM/wtC" -b feat-c

step "2. each worker creates its own SPEC (writes doc + one live file, nothing shared)"
for W in A B C; do
  w=$(echo "$W" | tr 'A-Z' 'a-z')
  D "$SIM/wt$W" new spec "Feature $W" --bucket "sim$w"
done

step "3. workers mark work + commit; A completes mid-sprint"
for W in A B C; do
  WT="$SIM/wt$W"
  SPECFILE=$(ls "$WT"/decree/spec/sim$(echo "$W" | tr 'A-Z' 'a-z')/*.md)
  # check every primary checkbox so 'complete' passes its 100% gate
  perl -pi -e 's/- \[ \]/- [x]/g' "$SPECFILE"
  git -C "$WT" add -A && git -C "$WT" commit -qm "spec $W"
done
SPEC_A_ID=$(grep -o 'SPEC-[0-9A-Z]*' "$SIM/wtA"/decree/spec/sim-a/*.md | head -1)
D "$SIM/wtA" sprint complete "$SPEC_A_ID"
git -C "$SIM/wtA" add -A && git -C "$SIM/wtA" commit -qm "complete $SPEC_A_ID"

step "4. same-worktree concurrency: two parallel 'new spec' in wtB"
( D "$SIM/wtB" new spec "Concurrent One" --bucket sim-b ) &
( D "$SIM/wtB" new spec "Concurrent Two" --bucket sim-b ) &
wait
LIVE_B=$(ls "$SIM/wtB"/decree/sprints/live/ | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
echo "live files in wtB: $LIVE_B (expect 3)"
git -C "$SIM/wtB" add -A && git -C "$SIM/wtB" commit -qm "concurrent specs"

step "5. orchestrator merges all three branches — MUST be conflict-free"
cd "$MAIN"
for BR in feat-a feat-b feat-c; do
  git merge -q --no-edit "$BR" || { echo "FATAL: merge conflict on $BR"; git status; exit 1; }
done

step "6. orchestrator supervision: lint + progress + status on integrated tree"
D "$MAIN" lint
D "$MAIN" sprint status
D "$MAIN" progress || true   # progress may exit nonzero only on real errors
D "$MAIN" progress --sprint "$(grep -o 'SPRINT-[0-9A-Z]*' decree/sprints/state.yaml | head -1)"

step "7. per-worktree inspection also works (orchestrator cd's into a worktree)"
D "$SIM/wtB" progress --changed --base main || D "$SIM/wtB" progress

step "8. rollover: outcomes only for OPEN items (A's is already resolved)"
OPEN_IDS=$(cd "$MAIN" && grep -L "outcome:" decree/sprints/live/*.yaml | xargs -n1 basename | sed 's/\.yaml//')
{
  echo "outcomes:"
  for ID in $OPEN_IDS; do
    printf '  %s:\n    kind: carried_over\n    reason: integration continues next sprint\n' "$ID"
  done
} > /tmp/outcomes-sim.yaml
D "$MAIN" sprint rollover "Sprint 2" --outcomes /tmp/outcomes-sim.yaml
test -n "$(ls "$MAIN"/decree/sprints/closed/)" || { echo "FATAL: no archive written"; exit 1; }
D "$MAIN" lint

step "ALL GREEN — parallel scenario proven. Sim dir: $SIM"

content.text:
**Uçtan uca kanıt yeşil.** Gerçek git worktree simülasyonu senaryonun tamamını doğruladı:

- 3 worker worktree kendi SPEC'ini + tek canlı üyelik dosyasını yazdı — ortak dosyaya sıfır dokunuş
- Worker A sprint ortasında `sprint complete` ile işini kapattı (yalnız kendi dosyasına yazarak)
- Aynı worktree'de iki eşzamanlı `new spec` yarışsız çalıştı (3 canlı dosya, kayıp yok)
- Orkestratör üç branch'i **çakışmasız** merge etti; entegre ağaçta lint 0 hata
- `sprint status` açık işleri ve "Done (awaiting rollover)" bölümünü ayrı gösterdi; varsayılan progress kapsamı tamamlanan item'ı dışarıda bıraktı, `--sprint <aktif>` dahil etti
- Rollover yalnız AÇIK item'lar için outcome istedi, arşiv yazıldı, lint yine temiz

İlk mesajdaki iki soru artık canlı kanıtlı: worker'lar kendi decree'lerinde çalışıp işaretleyebiliyor ve bitirebiliyor; orkestratör hem worktree-içi hem entegrasyon görünümünden denetleyebiliyor.

Test + doküman workflow'u (6 paralel ajan) hâlâ koşuyor — bitince tam gate seti (pytest/ruff/lint/towncrier) ve adversarial review fazına geçeceğim.

4	
5	| Module | Role | Key rule |
6	|--------|------|----------|
7	| [config.py](config.py) | Loads `decree.toml`, discovers project root | Single source of truth for all format rules. Uses `lru_cache` — tests must call `cache_clear()` |
8	| [parser.py](parser.py) | `DocFrontmatter` + `DocDocument` (Pydantic v2) | **Only module that reads/writes document files.** All other modules receive parsed objects |
9	| [doctypes.py](doctypes.py) | `DocType` frozen dataclass | Defines canonical ID pattern, lifecycle, transitions. Migration-only numeric handling lives in `commands/migrate.py` |
10	| [validators.py](validators.py) | Cross-file integrity + cross-type references | Pure functions: take doc lists, return error strings. No file I/O |
11	| [c4.py](c4.py) | C4 validation + Mermaid C4Container diagrams | Opt-in: only activated when `[types.*.c4]` exists in config. Reads `raw_metadata` for C4 fields |
12	| [sprints.py](sprints.py) | Sprint directory store at `decree/sprints/` (`state.yaml` + `live/<DOC-ID>.yaml` + `closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml`) | Writes touch exactly one file; items with an outcome are not live membership |
13	| [cli.py](cli.py) | argparse entry point | Dispatches to `commands/` modules |
14	| [log.py](log.py) | `info()`, `error()`, `success()`, `fail()` | All output goes to stderr. Stdout is reserved for machine-readable output |
15	| [template.py](template.py) | Fills template placeholders for `decree new` | Appends required sections not already in the template |
16	| [model_diagram.py](model_diagram.py) | Graphviz DOT generator for the document model | Output: `docs/model.png`. Run with `uv run python -m decree.model_diagram` |
17	
18	## Commands
19	
20	Each file in `commands/` is one CLI subcommand. All export a `run(args)` function returning an exit code.
...
22	|---------|------|-------|
23	| `new` | [commands/new.py](commands/new.py) | Generates `TYPE-ULID`, slugifies title, stamps date; derived indexes are explicit |
24	| `status` | [commands/status.py](commands/status.py) | Enforces transition rules. Supersede links both docs bidirectionally |
25	| `lint` | [commands/lint.py](commands/lint.py) | Aggregates errors from `validators.py` + `c4.py`. `--check-attachments` is opt-in |
26	| `index` | [commands/index.py](commands/index.py) | Generates markdown table + `GRAPH_MARKER` per type. `graph.py` imports the marker from here |
27	| `graph` | [commands/graph.py](commands/graph.py) | Appends Mermaid diagrams below the marker. Auto-runs `index` if marker is missing |
28	| `progress` | [commands/progress.py](commands/progress.py) | Counts primary `- [x]` / `- [ ]` checkboxes, with all/doc/chain/changed/governs scopes and separate deferred counts |
29	| `ddd` | [commands/ddd.py](commands/ddd.py) | Lifecycle assessment and next action; supports doc/chain/changed/governs scopes |
30	| `sprint` | [commands/sprint.py](commands/sprint.py) | `init`/`status`/`add`/`backlog`/`draft-pool`/`complete`/`drop`/`pause`/`resume`/`rollover` over the `decree/sprints/` directory store; `complete`/`drop` record item-level outcomes mid-sprint |
31	| `report` | [commands/report.py](commands/report.py) | Explicit completion-report regeneration; no hidden refresh during lint |
32	| `commit` | [commands/commit.py](commands/commit.py) | Git commit wrapper for canonical `Implements:`/`Refs:`/`Fixes:` trailers |
33	| `migrate` | [commands/migrate.py](commands/migrate.py) | Explicit corpus migrations: `audit-coherence`, `governs`, `ids`, and `sprint-ledger` (v1 `ledger.yaml` monolith → v2 directory store) |
34	| `health` / `stale` | [commands/health.py](commands/health.py) | Stale decisions, ungoverned hotspots, dead-governance (findings), advisory suggested-governance — reads `observed_governs`; never feeds `queries.py`. See [health-signals.md](../../docs/health-signals.md) |
35	| `why` / `refs` | [commands/queries.py](commands/queries.py) | SQLite-index-backed governance queries; never silently re-parse markdown |
36	| `mcp serve` | [commands/mcp_server.py](commands/mcp_server.py) | FastMCP server exposing `why`/`refs`/`stale`/`health`/`intent_check`/`intent_review`/`progress`/`report` as agent tools; thin wrappers over command cores, no duplicate query logic |
37	
38	## Data Flow
39	
40	```
41	decree.toml → config.py → DocType instances

1	# decree
2	
3	Software decision lifecycle toolkit — PRDs, ADRs, SPECs with cross-type references, status enforcement, and validation.
4	
5	## Agent Entry Point
6	
7	Read [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) first. It is the tool-agnostic onboarding file for
8	new LLM sessions. This CLAUDE file remains as a Claude-specific quick reference.
9	
10	## Quick Reference
11	
12	```bash
13	decree new prd "Feature X"        # create PRD
14	decree new adr "Use Redis"        # create ADR
15	decree new spec "Cache Layer"     # create SPEC
16	decree status PRD-01KT22NMRTFTWFFARAN0PVEETA approve  # transition status
17	decree lint                       # validate all types + cross-refs
18	decree lint --check-attachments   # also validate attachment paths
19	decree index regenerate           # regenerate per-type indexes
20	decree index rebuild              # rebuild SQLite query cache
21	decree graph                      # Mermaid diagrams in index.md
22	decree progress                   # checkbox completion tracking
23	decree progress --changed --base origin/main  # scoped progress for parallel work
24	decree ddd --governs src/foo.py   # scoped lifecycle guidance
25	decree sprint init "Sprint 1"     # enable sprint mode (creates decree/sprints/state.yaml)
26	decree sprint complete SPEC-01KT22NMS0D19VMD8VPK4D2MNX  # record completed outcome mid-sprint (needs 100% primary ACs)
27	decree migrate sprint-ledger --apply  # convert v1 ledger.yaml to the v2 directory store
28	decree why src/foo.py             # path -> governing decisions
29	decree refs SPEC-01KT22NMS0D19VMD8VPK4D2MNX  # reverse graph for one decision
30	decree --version                  # installed package version
31	```
32	
33	## Architecture
34	
35	See [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md) for module responsibilities.
36	
37	```
38	src/decree/
39	  config.py      ← loads decree.toml, single source of truth
40	  parser.py      ← DocFrontmatter + DocDocument (Pydantic v2), only module that reads files
41	  validators.py  ← cross-file integrity + cross-type reference validation
42	  cli.py         ← argparse entry point
43	  commands/      ← one module per command namespace (new, status, lint, index, graph, progress, ddd, report, queries, ...)
44	```
45	
46	Details: [src/decree/CLAUDE.md](src/decree/CLAUDE.md)
47	
48	## Config
49	
50	All config in `decree.toml` (not pyproject.toml). See [docs/configuration.md](docs/configuration.md) for full schema.
51	
52	## Key Design Decisions
53	
54	- `config.py` is the single source of truth for format rules
55	- `parser.py` is the only module that touches document files on disk
56	- Canonical document IDs are frontmatter `TYPE-ULID`; `decree migrate ids` converts legacy numeric corpora.
57	- `warn_on_reference` (dead statuses) != terminal statuses — "implemented" is terminal but healthy to reference
58	- Staleness is direct-only, not transitive
59	- Provenance is two-layered: git guarantees commit→files (deterministic, content-addressed); commit→decision is the `Implements:/Refs:/Fixes:` trailer **convention**, not a git guarantee. Git-derived signals (`commits`, staleness, observed/dead governance) are deterministic to compute but advisory + coverage-honest, and never feed `why()` ([docs/provenance-model.md](docs/provenance-model.md))
60	- C4 is coupled in `c4.py`, not pluggable ([decree/adr/REDACTED.md](decree/adr/REDACTED.md))
61	- Sprint membership is one `live/<DOC-ID>.yaml` per document — items with an outcome are not live, and live items are sprint-agnostic until a rollover folds them into `closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml`, so parallel worktrees merge without conflicts ([decree/adr/sprints/REDACTED.md](decree/adr/sprints/REDACTED.md))
62	
63	## Development
64	
65	```bash
66	uv run pytest -q                                      # tests
67	uv run ruff check src/ tests/                         # lint
68	uv run ruff format src/ tests/                        # format
69	lychee --config .lychee.toml --no-progress '**/*.md'  # online markdown link check
70	uv run towncrier check --staged                       # require changelog fragment for changes
71	uv run decree lint                                    # validate dogfood docs
72	```
73	
74	Pre-commit runs ruff, lychee, Towncrier, and pytest automatically. CI runs pytest on Python 3.11-3.13, plus ruff, lychee, and Towncrier checks.
75	
76	See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for developer guidelines — rules for adding commands, extending config, writing tests, and code style.
77	
78	## Testing
79	
80	See [tests/CLAUDE.md](tests/CLAUDE.md) for test conventions and fixtures.
81	
82	## Dogfooding
83	
84	Decree manages its own decisions in `decree/`:
85	
86	- [PRD-01KT22NMRR63TXR7NX5XYRG5FK](decree/prd/prd-01kt22nmrr63txr7nx5xyrg5fk-c4-architecture-support.md): C4 Architecture Support
87	- [ADR-01KT22NMRV7GMAXKWSBEEN68KE](decree/adr/REDACTED.md): Coupled C4 module vs plugin architecture
88	- [SPEC-01KT22NMRWENYKC3MGRA50M7GE](decree/spec/spec-01kt22nmrwenykc3mgra50m7ge-c4-validation-and-diagram-generation.md): C4 validation and diagram generation

✓ 48 documents validated. 0 errors.
## v2.1.0 - 2026-07-03

### Breaking Changes

- Replace the monolithic sprint ledger `decree/sprints/ledger.yaml` with a
  directory store (`state.yaml` plus one `live/<DOC-ID>.yaml` file per
  membership and one `closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml` archive per closed sprint);
  existing projects must run `decree migrate sprint-ledger --apply` once.

### Features

- Add `decree sprint complete` and `decree sprint drop` to record item-level
  outcomes mid-sprint; the one-file-per-membership sprint store makes parallel
  worktree development conflict-free.

content.text:
Ultracode dedin, ben de gaza bastım 😄 Evet — **implementasyon bitti ve doğrulandı.** Durum:

- **Kod**: `sprints.py` dizin-store'a çevrildi (state.yaml + live/ + closed/), `sprint complete`/`drop` ve `migrate sprint-ledger` eklendi, tüm tüketiciler (new, lint, progress, ddd, generate-html, CLI + help) uyarlandı
- **Testler**: 774/774 yeşil (26'sı yeniden yazılan sprint testleri + migrasyon + paralel simülasyon dahil), ruff temiz
- **Dogfood**: PRD + ADR (eskisi supersede edildi) + SPEC decree'nin kendisiyle yaratıldı; repo'nun kendi ledger'ı `migrate sprint-ledger --apply` ile taşındı; `decree lint` 48 doküman 0 hata
- **Gerçek kanıt**: 3 git worktree + orkestratör simülasyonu uçtan uca yeşil — çakışmasız merge, mid-sprint complete, entegrasyon lint'i, rollover
- **Dokümanlar**: usage.md, index.md, architecture.md, CLAUDE/AGENTS dosyaları, skill (iki kopya), plan notu, changelog fragmentleri — hepsi güncel

Tek açık iş: **adversarial review workflow'u hâlâ arka planda koşuyor** (6 boyutlu bul→çürüt hattı — paused-v1-migrasyonu gibi kenar durumları tarıyor). O bitince onaylanmış bulgu varsa düzeltip SPEC'in kabul kriterlerini işaretleyeceğim ve `decree sprint complete SPEC-01KWKXHERB56W94SCRZEVMBQMJ` ile dogfood döngüsünü kapatacağım.

Yani: özü hazır ve çalışıyor; son kalite taraması + seremonik kapanış kaldı. Review sonucu gelince haber vereceğim.

input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
import json
p = '/Users/doruk/.claude/projects/-Users-doruk-Desktop-SIDE-HUSTLE-decree/9aba7607-c379-42b0-82e5-f238fc1a47cb/subagents/workflows/wf_56c54d0d-70b/journal.jsonl'
finders, verdicts = [], []
for line in open(p):
    try: rec = json.loads(line)
    except: continue
    if rec.get('type') != 'result': continue
    key = rec.get('key','')
    val = rec.get('result')
    if not isinstance(val, dict): continue
    if 'findings' in val:
        for f in val.get('findings', []):
            finders.append((key, f.get('severity'), f.get('title'), f.get('file',''), (f.get('failure_scenario') or '')[:200]))
    elif 'refuted' in val:
        verdicts.append((key[:100], val.get('refuted'), (val.get('reasoning') or '')[:200]))
print(f"FINDER RESULTS ({len(finders)} findings):")
for k,s,t,fl,fs in finders:
    print(f"\n[{s}] {t}")
    print(f"   file: {fl}")
    print(f"   scenario: {fs}")
print(f"\nVERDICTS ({len(verdicts)}):")
for k,r,why in verdicts:
    print(f"  refuted={r} :: {k}")
    print(f"     {why}")
EOF

content.subject: Faz 0: Recon — sprint/ledger envanteri (workflow)
content.description: 5 paralel ajanla CLI yüzeyi, sprints API tüketicileri, test envanteri, doküman envanteri, dogfood durumu. Workflow: wf_d75bd460-e4d

content.subject: Faz 1: Tasarımı kesinleştir (recon sonuçlarıyla)
content.description: sprint-v2-design.md'yi recon bulgularıyla finalize et: mevcut sprint subcommand seti, mid-sprint outcome kümesi, test/doküman listeleri.

content.subject: Faz 2: Decree dogfood — PRD + ADR (supersede) + SPEC
content.description: decree CLI ile: yeni PRD, eski ADR-01KW1QGHBT...'yi supersede eden yeni ADR, kabul kriterli yeni SPEC; SPEC aktif sprint'e girer (v1 yoluyla son kez).

content.subject: Faz 3: Çekirdek implementasyon — sprints.py v2 + komutlar
content.description: Dizin-ledger storage (state/live/closed), LedgerView, hedefli yazma API'si, lock, sprint complete/drop, migrate sprint-ledger, tüm tüketici modüllerin uyarlanması, CLI + help.

content.subject: Faz 4: Testler — v2 + migrasyon + paralel simülasyon
content.description: Storage round-trip, tüm operasyonlar, migrasyon eşdeğerliği, v1-tespit hata yolu, her invariant ±, paralel union simülasyonu, lock testi, CLI help testleri.

content.subject: Faz 5: Dokümantasyon + help + changelog
content.description: usage.md, README/AGENTS/CLAUDE.md'ler, docs/architecture.md, plans notu, towncrier fragment, argparse help metinleri. Repo'nun kendi ledger'ını migrate et (dogfood).

content.subject: Faz 6: Adversarial review + uçtan uca doğrulama
content.description: Çok boyutlu review workflow (bul→doğrula), pytest/ruff/lint/towncrier, 3-worktree gerçek git simülasyonu, SPEC'i decree sprint complete ile kapat.

348	def load_view(root: Path | None = None) -> LedgerView:
349	    """Assemble the full directory store; raises on the first malformed file."""
350	    state = load_state(root)
351	    live_entries, live_errors = _read_live_files(root)
352	    closed_records, closed_errors = _read_closed_files(root)
353	    problems = live_errors + closed_errors
354	    if problems:
355	        raise SprintLedgerError(problems[0])
356	    return LedgerView(
357	        state=state,
358	        live={item.document: item for _, item in live_entries},
359	        closed=tuple(record for _, record in closed_records),
360	    )
361	
362	
363	def save_state(state: SprintState, root: Path | None = None) -> None:
364	    """Atomically rewrite state.yaml. Callers hold ``_ledger_lock`` for transitions."""
365	    path = state_path(root)
366	    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
367	    _atomic_write_yaml(path, state.to_dict(), prefix=".state.")
368	
369	
370	def create_live_item(item: LiveItem, root: Path | None = None) -> None:
371	    """Create live/<DOC-ID>.yaml; O_EXCL makes the exists-check atomic."""
372	    directory = live_path(root)
373	    directory.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
374	    path = directory / f"{item.document}.yaml"
375	    rendered = yaml.safe_dump(_strip_none(item.to_dict()), sort_keys=False)
376	    try:
377	        fd = os.open(path, os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL | os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
378	    except FileExistsError:
379	        raise SprintLedgerError(_duplicate_live_message(item.document, path)) from None
380	    with os.fdopen(fd, "w") as handle:
381	        handle.write(rendered)
382	
383	
384	def rewrite_live_item(item: LiveItem, root: Path | None = None) -> None:
385	    """Atomically rewrite one existing live file (used by complete/drop)."""
386	    path = live_path(root) / f"{item.document}.yaml"
387	    _atomic_write_yaml(path, item.to_dict(), prefix=f".{item.document}.")
388	
389	
390	def remove_live_item(doc_id: str, root: Path | None = None) -> None:
391	    """Remove one live file. Fold-time only (rollover/pause)."""
392	    (live_path(root) / f"{require_doc_id(doc_id)}.yaml").unlink(missing_ok=True)
393	
394	
395	def write_closed_sprint(record: SprintRecord, root: Path | None = None) -> None:
396	    """Create closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml; archives are append-only, never rewritten."""
397	    directory = closed_path(root)
398	    directory.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
399	    path = directory / f"{record.id}.yaml"
400	    rendered = yaml.safe_dump(_strip_none(record.to_dict()), sort_keys=False)
401	    try:
402	        fd = os.open(path, os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL | os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
403	    except FileExistsError:
404	        raise SprintLedgerError(f"closed sprint archive already exists: {CLOSED_REL_PATH / path.name}") from None
405	    with os.fdopen(fd, "w") as handle:
406	        handle.write(rendered)
407	
408	
409	@contextmanager
410	def _ledger_lock(root: Path | None = None):
411	    """Advisory exclusive lock for composite state transitions.
412	
413	    Without ``fcntl`` (non-POSIX platforms) this is a documented no-op:
414	    single-file writes stay atomic, only transition composites lose mutual
415	    exclusion.
416	    """
417	    try:
418	        import fcntl
419	    except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover - platform without fcntl
420	        yield
421	        return
422	    directory = sprints_path(root)
423	    directory.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
424	    fd = os.open(directory / ".lock", os.O_CREAT | os.O_RDWR, 0o644)
425	    try:
426	        fcntl.flock(fd, fcntl.LOCK_EX)
427	        yield
428	    finally:
429	        os.close(fd)
430	
431	
432	def _read_live_files(root: Path | None) -> tuple[list[tuple[str, LiveItem]], list[str]]:
433	    entries: list[tuple[str, LiveItem]] = []
434	    errors: list[str] = []
435	    directory = live_path(root)
436	    if not directory.exists():
437	        return entries, errors
438	    for path in sorted(directory.glob("*.yaml")):
439	        where = (LIVE_REL_PATH / path.name).as_posix()
440	        try:
441	            raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text())
442	        except yaml.YAMLError as e:
443	            errors.append(f"{where}: invalid YAML: {e}")
444	            continue
445	        try:
446	            entries.append((path.name, LiveItem.from_raw(raw, where=where)))
447	        except SprintLedgerError as e:
448	            errors.append(str(e))
449	    return entries, errors
450	
451	
452	def _read_closed_files(root: Path | None) -> tuple[list[tuple[str, SprintRecord]], list[str]]:
453	    # Sorted filenames give chronological order: SPRINT-<ULID> is ULID-lexicographic.
454	    records: list[tuple[str, SprintRecord]] = []
455	    errors: list[str] = []
456	    directory = closed_path(root)
457	    if not directory.exists():
458	        return records, errors
459	    for path in sorted(directory.glob("*.yaml")):
460	        where = (CLOSED_REL_PATH / path.name).as_posix()
461	        try:
462	            raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text())
463	        except yaml.YAMLError as e:
464	            errors.append(f"{where}: invalid YAML: {e}")
465	            continue
466	        try:
467	            records.append((path.name, SprintRecord.from_raw(raw, where=where)))
468	        except SprintLedgerError as e:
469	            errors.append(str(e))
470	    return records, errors

473	def _duplicate_live_message(doc_id: str, path: Path) -> str:
474	    try:
475	        raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text())
476	    except (OSError, yaml.YAMLError):
477	        raw = None
478	    scope = str(raw.get("scope", "")).strip() if isinstance(raw, dict) else ""
479	    outcome = raw.get("outcome") if isinstance(raw, dict) else None
480	    if outcome is not None:
481	        kind = str(outcome.get("kind", "")).strip() if isinstance(outcome, dict) else ""
482	        detail = f" ({kind})" if kind else ""
483	        return f"{doc_id} already has a resolved live record{detail}; resolved record folds at next rollover"
484	    labels = {"active": "active sprint", "backlog": "backlog", "draft_pool": "draft_pool"}

state = SprintState.from_raw(raw)
    if state.schema != SCHEMA:
        raise SprintLedgerError(f"{STATE_REL_PATH}: schema must be {SCHEMA!r}")
    if state.mode != MODE_ENABLED:
        raise SprintLedgerError(f"{STATE_REL_PATH}: mode must be {MODE_ENABLED!r}")
    return state


def load_view(root: Path | None = None) -> LedgerView:
    """Assemble the full directory store; raises on the first malformed file."""
    state = load_state(root)
    live_entries, live_errors = _read_live_files(root)
    closed_records, closed_errors = _read_closed_files(root)
    problems = live_errors + closed_errors
    if problems:
        raise SprintLedgerError(problems[0])
    return LedgerView(
        state=state,
        live={item.document: item for _, item in live_entries},
        closed=tuple(record for _, record in closed_records),
    )


def save_state(state: SprintState, root: Path | None = None) -> None:
    """Atomically rewrite state.yaml. Callers hold ``_ledger_lock`` for transitions."""
    path = state_path(root)
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    _atomic_write_yaml(path, state.to_dict(), prefix=".state.")


def create_live_item(item: LiveItem, root: Path | None = None) -> None:
    """Create live/<DOC-ID>.yaml; O_EXCL makes the exists-check atomic."""
    directory = live_path(root)
    directory.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    path = directory / f"{item.document}.yaml"
    rendered = yaml.safe_dump(_strip_none(item.to_dict()), sort_keys=False)
    try:
        fd = os.open(path, os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL | os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
    except FileExistsError:
        raise SprintLedgerError(_duplicate_live_message(item.document, path)) from None
    with os.fdopen(fd, "w") as handle:
        handle.write(rendered)


def rewrite_live_item(item: LiveItem, root: Path | None = None) -> None:
    """Atomically rewrite one existing live file (used by complete/drop)."""
    path = live_path(root) / f"{item.document}.yaml"
    _atomic_write_yaml(path, item.to_dict(), prefix=f".{item.document}.")


def remove_live_item(doc_id: str, root: Path | None = None) -> None:
    """Remove one live file. Fold-time only (rollover/pause)."""
    (live_path(root) / f"{require_doc_id(doc_id)}.yaml").unlink(missing_ok=True)


def write_closed_sprint(record: SprintRecord, root: Path | None = None) -> None:
    """Create closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml; archives are append-only, never rewritten."""
    directory = closed_path(root)
    directory.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    path = directory / f"{record.id}.yaml"
    rendered = yaml.safe_dump(_strip_none(record.to_dict()), sort_keys=False)
    try:
        fd = os.open(path, os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL | os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
    except FileExistsError:
        raise SprintLedgerError(f"closed sprint archive already exists: {CLOSED_REL_PATH / path.name}") from None
    with os.fdopen(fd, "w") as handle:
        handle.write(rendered)


@contextmanager
def _ledger_lock(root: Path | None = None):
    """Advisory exclusive lock for composite state transitions.

    Without ``fcntl`` (non-POSIX platforms) this is a documented no-op:
    single-file writes stay atomic, only transition composites lose mutual
    exclusion.
    """
    try:
        import fcntl
    except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover - platform without fcntl
        yield
        return
    directory = sprints_path(root)
    directory.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    fd = os.open(directory / ".lock", os.O_CREAT | os.O_RDWR, 0o644)
    try:
        fcntl.flock(fd, fcntl.LOCK_EX)
        yield
    finally:
        os.close(fd)


def _read_live_files(root: Path | None) -> tuple[list[tuple[str, LiveItem]], list[str]]:
    entries: list[tuple[str, LiveItem]] = []
    errors: list[str] = []
    directory = live_path(root)
    if not directory.exists():
        return entries, errors
    for path in sorted(directory.glob("*.yaml")):
        where = (LIVE_REL_PATH / path.name).as_posix()
        try:
            raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text())
        except yaml.YAMLError as e:
            errors.append(f"{where}: invalid YAML: {e}")
            continue
        try:
            entries.append((path.name, LiveItem.from_raw(raw, where=where)))
        except SprintLedgerError as e:
            errors.append(str(e))
    return entries, errors


def _read_closed_files(root: Path | None) -> tuple[list[tuple[str, SprintRecord]], list[str]]:
    # Sorted filenames give chronological order: SPRINT-<ULID> is ULID-lexicographic.
    records: list[tuple[str, SprintRecord]] = []
    errors: list[str] = []
    directory = closed_path(root)
    if not directory.exists():
        return records, errors
    for path in sorted(directory.glob("*.yaml")):
        where = (CLOSED_REL_PATH / path.name).as_posix()
        try:
            raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text())
        except yaml.YAMLError as e:
            errors.append(f"{where}: invalid YAML: {e}")
            continue
        try:
            records.append((path.name, SprintRecord.from_raw(raw, where=where)))
        except SprintLedgerError as e:
            errors.append(str(e))
    return records, errors


def _duplicate_live_message(doc_id: str, path: Path) -> str:
    try:
        raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text())
    except (OSError, yaml.YAMLError):
        raw = None
    scope = str(raw.get("scope", "")).strip() if isinstance(raw, dict) else ""
    outcome = raw.get("outcome") if isinstance(raw, dict) else None
    if outcome is not None:
        kind = str(outcome.get("kind", "")).strip() if isinstance(outcome, dict) else ""
        detail = f" ({kind})" if kind else ""
        return f"{doc_id} already has a resolved live record{detail}; resolved record folds at next rollover"
    labels = {"active": "active sprint", "backlog": "backlog", "draft_pool": "draft_pool"}
    return f"{doc_id} is already in {labels.get(scope, scope or 'a live sprint file')}"


def _atomic_write_yaml(path: Path, data: dict[str, Any], *, prefix: str) -> None:
    rendered = yaml.safe_dump(_strip_none(data), sort_keys=False)
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile("w", dir=path.parent, prefix=prefix, suffix=".tmp", delete=False) as tmp:
        tmp.write(rendered)
        tmp_path = Path(tmp.name)
    tmp_path.replace(path)


# ─── operations ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def init_ledger(name: str, *, root: Path | None = None, today: str | None = None) -> SprintState:
    if state_path(root).exists():
        raise SprintLedgerError("sprint ledger already exists")
    _require_no_v1_ledger(root)
    state = SprintState(
        schema=SCHEMA,
        mode=MODE_ENABLED,
        state="active",
        active={"id": generate_sprint_id(), "name": name, "started": today or _today()},
        paused=None,
    )
    with _ledger_lock(root):
        live_path(root).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        closed_path(root).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        save_state(state, root=root)
    return state


def add_to_active_sprint(
    document: str,
    *,
    kind: str = "execution",
    source: str = "manual",
    root: Path | None = None,
    today: str | None = None,
) -> LiveItem:
    state = load_state(root)
    if state.state != "active" or not state.active:
        raise SprintLedgerError("sprint ledger is paused; use --backlog or --draft-pool")
    _validate_item_kind(kind)
    item = LiveItem(
        document=require_doc_id(document),
        scope="active",
        kind=kind,
        source=source,
        added=today or _today(),
    )
    create_live_item(item, root=root)
    return item

def complete_item(
    document: str,
    *,
    commits: tuple[str, ...] = (),
    root: Path | None = None,
    today: str | None = None,
) -> LiveItem:
    """Record a completed outcome on one live active item, touching only its file."""
    item = _open_active_item(document, root)
    snapshot = _snapshot_for_doc(_find_doc(item.document))
    incomplete = snapshot["primary_total"] > 0 and snapshot["primary_done"] != snapshot["primary_total"]
    if incomplete:
        raise SprintLedgerError(f"{item.document} cannot be completed unless primary acceptance criteria are 100%")
    outcome = {
        "kind": "completed",
        "at": today or _today(),
        "reason": None,
        "to_sprint": None,
        "to_document": None,
        "evidence": {"commits": list(commits)},
        "snapshot": snapshot,
    }
    updated = replace(item, outcome=outcome)
    rewrite_live_item(updated, root=root)
    return updated


def drop_item(
    document: str,
    *,
    reason: str,
    root: Path | None = None,
    today: str | None = None,
) -> LiveItem:
    """Record a dropped outcome on one live active item, touching only its file."""
    clean_reason = _require_reason(reason)
    item = _open_active_item(document, root)
    outcome = {
        "kind": "dropped",
        "at": today or _today(),
        "reason": clean_reason,
        "to_sprint": None,
        "to_document": None,
        "evidence": {"commits": []},
        "snapshot": _snapshot_for_doc(_find_doc(item.document)),
    }
    updated = replace(item, outcome=outcome)
    rewrite_live_item(updated, root=root)
    return updated


def pause_ledger(reason: str, *, root: Path | None = None, today: str | None = None) -> SprintState:
    clean_reason = _require_reason(reason)
    with _ledger_lock(root):
        view = load_view(root)
        if view.state.state != "active" or not view.state.active:
            raise SprintLedgerError("sprint ledger is not active")
        if view.active_open_items:
            raise SprintLedgerError(
                "cannot pause with open active-sprint items; complete, drop, or rollover them first"
            )
        today_str = today or _today()
        active = view.state.active
        record = SprintRecord(
            id=active["id"],
            name=active["name"],
            status="closed",
            started=active["started"],
            closed=today_str,
            items=tuple(_fold_item(item) for item in view.active_items),
        )
        write_closed_sprint(record, root=root)
        for item in view.active_items:
            remove_live_item(item.document, root=root)
        updated = replace(view.state, state="paused", active=None, paused={"since": today_str, "reason": clean_reason})
        save_state(updated, root=root)
    return updated


def resume_ledger(name: str, *, root: Path | None = None, today: str | None = None) -> SprintState:
    with _ledger_lock(root):
        state = load_state(root)
        if state.state != "paused":
            raise SprintLedgerError("sprint ledger is not paused")
        updated = replace(
            state,
            state="active",
            active={"id": generate_sprint_id(), "name": name, "started": today or _today()},
            paused=None,
        )
        save_state(updated, root=root)
    return updated


def rollover_ledger(
    name: str,
    outcomes: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
    docs: list[Any],
    *,
    root: Path | None = None,

def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "schema": self.schema,
            "mode": self.mode,
            "state": self.state,
            "active": self.active,
            "paused": self.paused,
        }


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class LiveItem:
    """Contents of one live/<DOC-ID>.yaml membership file."""

    document: str
    scope: str
    kind: str = "execution"
    source: str = "manual"
    added: str = field(default_factory=lambda: _today())
    since: str | None = None
    reason: str | None = None
    review_after: str | None = None
    carryover_from: str | None = None
    outcome: dict[str, Any] | None = None

    @classmethod
    def from_raw(cls, raw: Any, *, where: str) -> LiveItem:
        data = _expect_mapping(raw, where)
        return cls(
            document=str(data.get("document", "")).strip().upper(),
            scope=str(data.get("scope", "")).strip(),
            kind=str(data.get("kind", "execution")).strip(),
            source=str(data.get("source", "")).strip(),
            added=_date_str(data.get("added", _today()), f"{where}.added"),
            since=_date_str(data.get("since"), f"{where}.since") if data.get("since") else None,
            reason=str(data.get("reason", "")).strip() or None,
            review_after=(
                _date_str(data.get("review_after"), f"{where}.review_after") if data.get("review_after") else None
            ),
            carryover_from=_optional_sprint_id(data.get("carryover_from")),
            outcome=_stringify_dates(data.get("outcome")),
        )

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "document": self.document,
            "scope": self.scope,
            "kind": self.kind,
            # Empty source (e.g. migrated v1 draft_pool items) is omitted, not written as ''.
            "source": self.source or None,
            "added": self.added,
            "since": self.since,
            "reason": self.reason,
            "review_after": self.review_after,
            "carryover_from": self.carryover_from,
            "outcome": self.outcome,
        }


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SprintItem:
    """One item inside a closed sprint archive (v1-compatible shape)."""

    document: str
    kind: str = "execution"
    source: str = "manual"
    added: str = field(default_factory=lambda: _today())
    carryover_from: str | None = None
    outcome: dict[str, Any] | None = None

    @classmethod
    def from_raw(cls, raw: Any, *, where: str) -> SprintItem:
        data = _expect_mapping(raw, where)
        return cls(
            document=str(data.get("document", "")).strip().upper(),
            kind=str(data.get("kind", "execution")).strip(),
            source=str(data.get("source", "manual")).strip(),
            added=_date_str(data.get("added", _today()), f"{where}.added"),
            carryover_from=_optional_sprint_id(data.get("carryover_from")),
            outcome=_stringify_dates(data.get("outcome")),
        )

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "document": self.document,
            "kind": self.kind,
            "source": self.source,
            "added": self.added,
            "carryover_from": self.carryover_from,
            "outcome": self.outcome,
        }


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SprintRecord:
    """One closed/<SPRINT-ID>.yaml archive (v1-compatible shape)."""

    id: str
    name: str
    status: str
    started: str
    closed: str | None = None
    items: tuple[SprintItem, ...] = ()

    @classmethod
    def from_raw(cls, raw: Any, *, where: str) -> SprintRecord:
        data = _expect_mapping(raw, where)
        return cls(
            id=str(data.get("id", "")).strip().upper(),
            name=str(data.get("name", "")).strip(),
            status=str(data.get("status", "")).strip(),
            started=_date_str(data.get("started", ""), f"{where}.started") if data.get("started") else "",
            closed=_date_str(data.get("closed"), f"{where}.closed") if data.get("closed") else None,
            items=tuple(
                SprintItem.from_raw(item, where=f"{where}.items[{idx}]")

def _atomic_write_yaml(path: Path, data: dict[str, Any], *, prefix: str) -> None:
    rendered = yaml.safe_dump(_strip_none(data), sort_keys=False)
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile("w", dir=path.parent, prefix=prefix, suffix=".tmp", delete=False) as tmp:
        tmp.write(rendered)
        tmp_path = Path(tmp.name)
    tmp_path.replace(path)
input.new_string:
def _atomic_write_yaml(path: Path, data: dict[str, Any], *, prefix: str) -> None:
    rendered = yaml.safe_dump(_strip_none(data), sort_keys=False)
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile("w", dir=path.parent, prefix=prefix, suffix=".tmp", delete=False) as tmp:
        tmp.write(rendered)
        tmp_path = Path(tmp.name)
    # NamedTemporaryFile creates 0600; match the 0644 used for O_EXCL creates.
    os.chmod(tmp_path, 0o644)
    tmp_path.replace(path)
